Railway Maintenance Domain\n    92â†’\n    93â†’### 1.1 ECM Framework (EU 2019/779)\n    94â†’\n    95â†’The **Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM)** framework establishes legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance. While Dubai Metro is outside EU jurisdiction, the ECM framework represents industry best practice.\n    96â†’\n    97â†’#### ECM Function Mapping\n    98â†’\n    99â†’| Function | Responsibility | MMS V1.0 Feature | Status |\n   100â†’|----------|----------------|------------------|--------|\n   101â†’| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking | Visualization only |\n   102â†’| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Interval definitions, plan visualization | Mock data display |\n   103â†’| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection | Conflict indicators |\n   104â†’| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order visualization, depot allocation | Visual representation |\n   105â†’\n   106â†’#### ECM Compliance Requirements\n   107â†’\n   108â†’| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS V1.0 Implementation |\n   109â†’|-------------|-------------|------------------------|\n   110â†’| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings |\n   111â†’| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Not implemented |\n   112â†’| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Not implemented |\n   113â†’| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI display |\n   114â†’\n   115â†’---\n   116â†’\n   117â†’### 1.2 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)\n   118â†’\n   119â†’**RAMS** = Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety\n   120â†’\n   121â†’EN 50126-1:2017 defines the railway RAMS lifecycle. MMS visualizes RAMS metrics.\n   122â†’\n   123â†’#### RAMS Metric Definitions\n   124â†’\n   125â†’| Aspect | Definition | MMS V1.0 Feature |\n   126â†’|--------|------------|------------------|\n   127â†’| **Reliability** | Probability of intended function under stated conditions | MTBF display |\n   128â†’| **Availability** | Ability to perform required function at given instant | Fleet availability % |\n   129â†’| **Maintainability** | Probability of maintenance within stated time | MTTR display |\n   130â†’| **Safety** | Freedom from unacceptable risk | Critical task priority |\n   131â†’\n   132â†’#### RAMS Targets\n   133â†’\n   134â†’| Metric | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |\n   135â†’|--------|---------|--------|-------------------|\n   136â†’| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) x 100 | >= 97% | < 95% |\n   137â†’| MTBF | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | Operator-defined | Operator-defined |\n   138â†’| MTTR | Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs | <= 24 hours | > 48 hours |\n   139â†’| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time / Scheduled) x 100 | >= 95% | < 90% |\n   140â†’\n   141â†’---\n   142â†’\n   143â†’### 1.3 EN 15380 Railway Terminology\n   144â†’\n   145â†’All user-facing labels MUST use EN 15380 compliant terminology.\n   146â†’\n   147â†’#### Mandatory Term Usage\n   148â†’\n   149â†’| EN 15380 Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |\n   150â†’|---------------|------------|----------------|\n   151â†’| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |\n   152â†’| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model | Group, Class, Collection |\n   153â†’| Depot | Designated maintenance facility | Garage, Workshop, Station |\n   154â†’| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |\n   155â†’| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |\n   156â†’| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |\n   157â†’| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet | Usage balancing |\n   158â†’\n   159â†’#### Maintenance Terminology\n   160â†’\n   161â†’| Term | Definition | Context |\n   162â†’|------|------------|---------|\n   163â†’| PM (Preventive Maintenance) | Scheduled maintenance at defined intervals | Time or mileage-based |\n   164â†’| CM (Corrective Maintenance) | Unscheduled repair after failure detection | Reactive |\n   165â†’| CBM (Condition-Based Maintenance) | Triggered by condition monitoring | Sensor-based |\n   166â†’| Overhaul | Major refurbishment restoring to original condition | Resets counters |\n   167â†’| Heavy Maintenance | Significant maintenance requiring extended depot stay | Multi-day duration |\n   168â†’\n   169â†’---\n   170â†’\n   171â†’## 2. Dubai Metro Context\n   172â†’\n   173â†’### 2.1 Fleet Composition\n   174â†’\n   175â†’| Fleet | Prefix | Count | Line | Depot | Daily Avg km |\n   176â†’|-------|--------|-------|------|-------|--------------|\n   177â†’| Alstom | ALT-001 to ALT-079 | 79 | Red Line | Al Rashidiya | 420-475 km/day |\n   178â†’| Kinki Sharyo | KNK-001 to KNK-049 | 49 | Green Line | Jebel Ali | 400-454 km/day |\n   179â†’| **Total** | | **128** | | | |\n   180â†’\n   181â†’#### Alstom Fleet Characteristics\n   182â†’\n   183â†’| Property | Value |\n   184â†’|----------|-------|\n   185â†’| Quantity | 79 trains |\n   186â†’| Train Designation | ALT-001 through ALT-079 |\n   187â†’| Cars per Train | 5 |\n   188â†’| Base Mileage Range | 450,000 - 800,000 km |\n   189â†’| Assigned Depot | Al Rashidiya |\n   190â†’\n   191â†’#### Kinki Sharyo Fleet Characteristics\n   192â†’\n   193â†’| Property | Value |\n   194â†’|----------|-------|\n   195â†’| Quantity | 49 trains (newer fleet) |\n   196â†’| Train Designation | KNK-001 through KNK-049 |\n   197â†’| Cars per Train | 5 |\n   198â†’| Base Mileage Range | 320,000 - 600,000 km |\n   199â†’| Assigned Depot | Jebel Ali |\n   200â†’\n   201â†’---\n   202â†’\n   203â†’### 2.2 Depot Configuration\n   204â†’\n   205â†’| Depot ID | Name | Tracks | Weekly Man-Hours | Serves |\n   206â†’|----------|------|--------|------------------|--------|\n   207â†’| depot-red | Al Rashidiya | 5 | 2,800 | Alstom fleet |\n   208â†’| depot-green | Jebel Ali | 4 | 2,400 | Kinki Sharyo fleet |\n   209â†’\n   210â†’#### Combined Resources\n   211â†’\n   212â†’| Resource | Value | Calculation |\n   213â†’|----------|-------|-------------|\n   214â†’| Total Maintenance Tracks | 9 | 5 + 4 |\n   215â†’| Weekly Man-Hours | 5,200 | 2,800 + 2,400 |\n   216â†’| Monthly Man-Hours | 20,800 | 5,200 x 4 weeks |\n   217â†’| Track Capacity (per day) | 2 trains per track | Industry standard |\n   218â†’| Daily Maintenance Capacity | 18 trains | 9 tracks x 2 trains |\n   219â†’\n   220â†’---\n   221â†’\n   222â†’### 2.3 Contract Scope\n   223â†’\n   224â†’| Parameter | Value |\n   225â†’|-----------|-------|\n   226â†’| Contract Duration | 12 years |\n   227â†’| Operator | Keolis MHI |\n   228â†’| Client | RTA Dubai |\n   229â†’| Total Rolling Stock | 128 trains |\n   230â†’| Planning Horizon | 1-12 years |\n   231â†’| Maintenance Levels | 4 (A/B/C/D checks) |\n   232â†’\n   233â†’---\n   234â†’\n   235â†’## 3. Data Model\n   236â†’\n   237â†’### 3.1 Train Entity\n   238â†’\n   239â†’```typescript\n   240â†’/**\n   241â†’ * Train entity - represents a single rolling stock unit.\n   242â†’ * JSON-serializable for persistence and API transport.\n   243â†’ */\n   244â†’export type AssetType = 'ALSTOM' | 'KINKI';\n   245â†’\n   246â†’export interface Train {\n   247â†’  /** Unique identifier: \"TRAIN-001\" or \"ALT-001\" */\n   248â†’  id: string;\n   249â†’\n   250â†’  /** Human-readable display name */\n   251â†’  name: string;\n   252â†’\n   253â†’  /**\n   254â†’   * Fleet type for grouping and maintenance profile\n   255â†’   * ALSTOM: Red Line fleet (79 trains)\n   256â†’   * KINKI: Green Line fleet (49 trains)\n   257â†’   */\n   258â†’  assetType: AssetType;\n   259â†’}\n   260â†’\n   261â†’/** Extended Train with optional fields */\n   262â†’export interface TrainExtended extends Train {\n   263â†’  carsCount?: number;\n   264â†’  currentMileageKm?: number;\n   265â†’  avgDailyMileageKm?: number;\n   266â†’  status?: 'operational' | 'maintenance' | 'out-of-service';\n   267â†’  depotId?: string;\n   268â†’}\n   269â†’```\n   270â†’\n   271â†’---\n   272â†’\n   273â†’### 3.2 Task Entity\n   274â†’\n   275â†’```typescript\n   276â†’/**\n   277â†’ * Task types for visual categorization.\n   278â†’ * Maps to color: maintenance=blue, overhaul=orange, inspection=gray\n   279â†’ */\n   280â†’export type TaskType = 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';\n   281â†’\n   282â†’/**\n   283â†’ * Maintenance level classification (A/B/C/D check system).\n   284â†’ */\n   285â†’export type MaintenanceLevel = 'A' | 'B' | 'C' | 'D';\n   286â†’\n   287â†’/**\n   288â†’ * Task entity - represents a scheduled maintenance activity.\n   289â†’ * Dates stored as ISO 8601 strings, NOT Date objects.\n   290â†’ */\n   291â†’export interface Task {\n   292â†’  /** Unique identifier: \"{trainId}-{year}-{index}\" */\n   293â†’  id: string;\n   294â†’\n   295â†’  /** Foreign key reference to Train.id */\n   296â†’  trainId: string;\n   297â†’\n   298â†’  /** Task start date in ISO 8601: \"YYYY-MM-DD\" */\n   299â†’  startDate: string;\n   300â†’\n   301â†’  /** Task end date in ISO 8601: \"YYYY-MM-DD\" */\n   302â†’  endDate: string;\n   303â†’\n   304â†’  /**\n   305â†’   * Visual category for color coding:\n   306â†’   * - maintenance: Blue (#1d4ed8)\n   307â†’   * - overhaul: Orange (#f59e0b)\n   308â†’   * - inspection: Gray (#6b7280)\n   309â†’   */\n   310â†’  type: TaskType;\n   311â†’\n   312â†’  /**\n   313â†’   * Conflict indicator flag.\n   314â†’   * True if task overlaps another or violates constraints.\n   315â†’   */\n   316â†’  hasConflict: boolean;\n   317â†’\n   318â†’  /** Optional: ID of task that must complete before this one */\n   319â†’  predecessorId?: string;\n   320â†’}\n   321â†’\n   322â†’/** Extended Task with optional fields */\n   323â†’export interface TaskExtended extends Task {\n   324â†’  maintenanceLevel?: MaintenanceLevel;\n   325â†’  manHours?: number;\n   326â†’  estimatedCost?: number;\n   327â†’  dependencies?: string[];\n   328â†’  status?: 'scheduled' | 'in-progress' | 'completed' | 'cancelled';\n   329â†’  depotId?: string;\n   330â†’  tracksRequired?: number;\n   331â†’}\n   332â†’```\n   333â†’\n   334â†’---\n   335â†’\n   336â†’### 3.3 TaskRender (Computed)\n   337â†’\n   338â†’```typescript\n   339â†’/**\n   340â†’ * Color mapping for task types.\n   341â†’ */\n   342â†’export type TaskColor = 'blue' | 'orange' | 'gray';\n   343â†’\n   344â†’export const TYPE_TO_COLOR: Record<TaskType, TaskColor> = {\n   345â†’  maintenance: 'blue',\n   346â†’  overhaul: 'orange',\n   347â†’  inspection: 'gray',\n   348â†’};\n   349â†’\n   350â†’/**\n   351â†’ * TaskRender - computed values for canvas rendering.\n   352â†’ * NEVER persisted - recomputed on data load.\n   353â†’ */\n   354â†’export interface TaskRender {\n   355â†’  id: string;\n   356â†’  trainIndex: number;     // Y position (0-based index)\n   357â†’  startDay: number;       // X position (days since start)\n   358â†’  duration: number;       // Width in days\n   359â†’  color: TaskColor;       // Derived from type\n   360â†’  hasConflict: boolean;\n   361â†’  type: TaskType;\n   362â†’  predecessorId?: string;\n   363â†’}\n   364â†’\n   365â†’/**\n   366â†’ * Transform persistent Task to render-ready TaskRender.\n   367â†’ */\n   368â†’function transformToRender(\n   369â†’  task: Task,\n   370â†’  trainIndexMap: Map<string, number>,\n   371â†’  datasetStartDate: string\n   372â†’): TaskRender {\n   373â†’  const trainIndex = trainIndexMap.get(task.trainId);\n   374â†’  if (trainIndex === undefined) {\n   375â†’    throw new Error(`Train not found: ${task.trainId}`);\n   376â†’  }\n   377â†’\n   378â†’  const startDay = daysBetween(datasetStartDate, task.startDate);\n   379â†’  const duration = daysBetween(task.startDate, task.endDate);\n   380â†’\n   381â†’  return {\n   382â†’    id: task.id,\n   383â†’    trainIndex,\n   384â†’    startDay,\n   385â†’    duration,\n   386â†’    color: TYPE_TO_COLOR[task.type],\n   387â†’    hasConflict: task.hasConflict,\n   388â†’    type: task.type,\n   389â†’    predecessorId: task.predecessorId,\n   390â†’  };\n   391â†’}\n   392â†’```\n   393â†’\n   394â†’---\n   395â†’\n   396â†’### 3.4 Conflict Representation\n   397â†’\n   398â†’#### Conflict Detection Formula\n   399â†’\n   400â†’```\n   401â†’Overlap Conflict:\n   402â†’  (A.startDay <= B.startDay + B.duration) AND\n   403â†’  (A.startDay + A.duration >= B.startDay) AND\n   404â†’  (A.trainId == B.trainId)\n   405â†’```\n   406â†’\n   407â†’#### Conflict Visualization\n   408â†’\n   409â†’| LOD Level | Conflict Indicator |\n   410â†’|-----------|-------------------|\n   411â†’| MACRO | Red intensity in heatmap cells |\n   412â†’| MESO | Red background on task block |\n   413â†’| MICRO | Red border + warning icon on task card |\n   414â†’\n   415â†’Color specification: Intent.DANGER red (`#cd4246`)\n   416â†’\n   417â†’---\n   418â†’\n   419â†’### 3.5 View Configuration\n   420â†’\n   421â†’```typescript\n   422â†’/**\n   423â†’ * Dataset metadata for world bounds calculation.\n   424â†’ */\n   425â†’export interface DatasetMeta {\n   426â†’  startDate: string;    // ISO 8601: \"2024-01-01\"\n   427â†’  trainCount: number;   // Number of trains\n   428â†’  years: number;        // Time range in years\n   429â†’}\n   430â†’\n   431â†’export const DEFAULT_DATASET_META: DatasetMeta = {\n   432â†’  startDate: '2024-01-01',\n   433â†’  trainCount: 50,\n   434â†’  years: 10,\n   435â†’};\n   436â†’\n   437â†’/**\n   438â†’ * Canvas view configuration constants.\n   439â†’ */\n   440â†’export interface ViewConfig {\n   441â†’  pxPerDay: number;        // Pixels per day (X scale)\n   442â†’  pxPerTrain: number;      // Pixels per train row (Y scale)\n   443â†’  minZoom: number;         // Minimum zoom level\n   444â†’  maxZoom: number;         // Maximum zoom level\n   445â†’  zoomSensitivity: number; // Scroll sensitivity\n   446â†’  lodThresholds: {\n   447â†’    macro: number;         // Below this = MACRO\n   448â†’    micro: number;         // Above this = MICRO\n   449â†’  };\n   450â†’}\n   451â†’\n   452â†’export const DEFAULT_VIEW_CONFIG: ViewConfig = {\n   453â†’  pxPerDay: 10,\n   454â†’  pxPerTrain: 24,\n   455â†’  minZoom: 0.1,\n   456â†’  maxZoom: 5.0,\n   457â†’  zoomSensitivity: 0.002,\n   458â†’  lodThresholds: {\n   459â†’    macro: 0.5,\n   460â†’    micro: 2.0,\n   461â†’  },\n   462â†’};\n   463â†’\n   464â†’/** Level of Detail enumeration */\n   465â†’export type LODLevel = 'MACRO' | 'MESO' | 'MICRO';\n   466â†’\n   467â†’/** Runtime viewport state */\n   468â†’export interface ViewportState {\n   469â†’  zoom: number;    // 0.1 to 5.0\n   470â†’  panX: number;    // Camera X (world units)\n   471â†’  panY: number;    // Camera Y (world units)\n   472â†’  lod: LODLevel;   // Derived from zoom\n   473â†’}\n   474â†’```\n   475â†’\n   476â†’---\n   477â†’\n   478â†’## 4. Mathematical Formulas\n   479â†’\n   480â†’### 4.1 Km Variance (Coefficient of Variation)\n   481â†’\n   482â†’Primary metric for fleet mileage balance. Lower variance = better distribution.\n   483â†’\n   484â†’```\n   485â†’Km Variance % = (Standard Deviation / Mean) * 100\n   486â†’\n   487â†’Where:\n   488â†’  mean = sum(all train km) / n\n   489â†’  stddev = sqrt(sum((x_i - mean)^2) / n)\n   490â†’```\n   491â†’\n   492â†’```typescript\n   493â†’function calculateKmVariance(kmValues: number[]): number {\n   494â†’  if (kmValues.length < 2) return 0;\n   495â†’\n   496â†’  const mean = kmValues.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / kmValues.length;\n   497â†’  if (mean === 0) return 0;\n   498â†’\n   499â†’  const squaredDiffs = kmValues.map(km => Math.pow(km - mean, 2));\n   500â†’  const variance = squaredDiffs.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / kmValues.length;\n   501â†’  const stdDev = Math.sqrt(variance);\n   502â†’\n   503â†’  return (stdDev / mean) * 100;\n   504â†’}\n   505â†’```\n   506â†’\n   507â†’| Status | Variance | Indicator |\n   508â†’|--------|----------|-----------|\n   509â†’| Green (Healthy) | <= 10% | Green badge |\n   510â†’| Yellow (Warning) | 10% - 15% | Yellow badge |\n   511â†’| Red (Critical) | > 15% | Red badge |\n   512â†’\n   513â†’---\n   514â†’\n   515â†’### 4.2 Fleet Availability\n   516â†’\n   517â†’```\n   518â†’Fleet Availability % = (Fit Trains / Total Trains) * 100\n   519â†’\n   520â†’Targets:\n   521â†’  Target: >= 97%\n   522â†’  Critical: < 95%\n   523â†’```\n   524â†’\n   525â†’```typescript\n   526â†’function calculateFleetAvailability(fitTrains: number, totalTrains: number): number {\n   527â†’  if (totalTrains === 0) return 0;\n   528â†’  return (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100;\n   529â†’}\n   530â†’```\n   531â†’\n   532â†’---\n   533â†’\n   534â†’### 4.3 MTBF / MTTR / MDBF\n   535â†’\n   536â†’```\n   537â†’MTBF = Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures (Hours)\n   538â†’MDBF = Total Operating km / Number of Failures (Kilometers)\n   539â†’MTTR = Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs (Hours)\n   540â†’       Target: <= 24 hours, Critical: > 48 hours\n   541â†’```\n   542â†’\n   543â†’```typescript\n   544â†’interface FailureMetrics {\n   545â†’  mtbf: number;\n   546â†’  mdbf: number;\n   547â†’  mttr: number;\n   548â†’}\n   549â†’\n   550â†’function calculateFailureMetrics(\n   551â†’  operatingHours: number,\n   552â†’  operatingKm: number,\n   553â†’  downtimeHours: number,\n   554â†’  failureCount: number,\n   555â†’  repairCount: number\n   556â†’): FailureMetrics {\n   557â†’  return {\n   558â†’    mtbf: failureCount > 0 ? operatingHours / failureCount : Infinity,\n   559â†’    mdbf: failureCount > 0 ? operatingKm / failureCount : Infinity,\n   560â†’    mttr: repairCount > 0 ? downtimeHours / repairCount : 0,\n   561â†’  };\n   562â†’}\n   563â†’```\n   564â†’\n   565â†’---\n   566â†’\n   567â†’### 4.4 Coordinate System\n   568â†’\n   569â†’#### Constants\n   570â†’\n   571â†’| Constant | Value | Description |\n   572â†’|----------|-------|-------------|\n   573â†’| `PX_PER_DAY` | 10 pixels | 1 day = 10 pixels on X axis |\n   574â†’| `PX_PER_TRAIN` | 24 pixels | 1 train row = 24 pixels on Y axis |\n   575â†’| `minZoom` | 0.1 | Minimum zoom (zoomed out) |\n   576â†’| `maxZoom` | 5.0 | Maximum zoom (zoomed in) |\n   577â†’\n   578â†’#### Coordinate Conversions\n   579â†’\n   580â†’```typescript\n   581â†’const PX_PER_DAY = 10;\n   582â†’const PX_PER_TRAIN = 24;\n   583â†’\n   584â†’function dayToX(day: number): number {\n   585â†’  return day * PX_PER_DAY;\n   586â†’}\n   587â†’\n   588â†’function trainIndexToY(index: number): number {\n   589â†’  return -index * PX_PER_TRAIN;\n   590â†’}\n   591â†’\n   592â†’function screenToWorld(\n   593â†’  screenX: number, screenY: number,\n   594â†’  cameraX: number, cameraY: number,\n   595â†’  zoom: number,\n   596â†’  viewportWidth: number, viewportHeight: number\n   597â†’): { worldX: number; worldY: number } {\n   598â†’  return {\n   599â†’    worldX: cameraX + (screenX - viewportWidth / 2) / zoom,\n   600â†’    worldY: cameraY + (screenY - viewportHeight / 2) / zoom,\n   601â†’  };\n   602â†’}\n   603â†’```\n   604â†’\n   605â†’#### LOD Thresholds\n   606â†’\n   607â†’| LOD Level | Zoom Range | Time Scale | Rendering |\n   608â†’|-----------|------------|------------|-----------|\n   609â†’| MACRO | < 0.5 | 10-20 years | Heatmap (aggregated) |\n   610â†’| MESO | 0.5 - 2.0 | 2-5 years | Task blocks (instanced) |\n   611â†’| MICRO | >= 2.0 | 4-8 weeks | Detailed cards (HTML) |\n   612â†’\n   613â†’#### Blend Zone\n   614â†’\n   615â†’```typescript\n   616â†’const BLEND_ZONE_SIZE = 0.15;\n   617â†’\n   618â†’function calculateOpacity(\n   619â†’  zoom: number,\n   620â†’  threshold: number,\n   621â†’  direction: 'in' | 'out'\n   622â†’): number {\n   623â†’  const t = (zoom - threshold) / BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;\n   624â†’  const clamped = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, t));\n   625â†’  return direction === 'in' ? clamped : 1 - clamped;\n   626â†’}\n   627â†’```\n   628â†’\n   629â†’---\n   630â†’\n   631â†’### 4.5 Km Penalty Calculation (Solver)\n   632â†’\n   633â†’Used by allocation solver to prioritize under-utilized trains.\n   634â†’\n   635â†’```\n   636â†’deviation_pct = |current_km - target_km| / max(target_km, 1) * 100\n   637â†’\n   638â†’If current_km < target_km:\n   639â†’  penalty = -deviation_pct * 10  (BONUS)\n   640â†’If current_km >= target_km:\n   641â†’  penalty = +deviation_pct * 10  (PENALTY)\n   642â†’```\n   643â†’\n   644â†’```typescript\n   645â†’function calculateKmPenalty(\n   646â†’  currentKm: number,\n   647â†’  targetKm: number,\n   648â†’  fleetAvgKm: number\n   649â†’): number {\n   650â†’  const effectiveTarget = targetKm || fleetAvgKm;\n   651â†’  if (effectiveTarget === 0) return 0;\n   652â†’\n   653â†’  const deviationPct = Math.abs(currentKm - effectiveTarget) / effectiveTarget * 100;\n   654â†’\n   655â†’  if (currentKm < effectiveTarget) {\n   656â†’    return -Math.round(deviationPct * 10);\n   657â†’  } else {\n   658â†’    return Math.round(deviationPct * 10);\n   659â†’  }\n   660â†’}\n   661â†’```\n   662â†’\n   663â†’---\n   664â†’\n   665â†’### 4.6 Haversine Distance (Dead Running)\n   666â†’\n   667â†’Great circle distance between two geographic points.\n   668â†’\n   669â†’```\n   670â†’a = sin^2(delta_lat/2) + cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin^2(delta_lon/2)\n   671â†’c = 2 * atan2(sqrt(a), sqrt(1-a))\n   672â†’distance = R * c\n   673â†’\n   674â†’Where: R = 6371 km (Earth's radius)\n   675â†’```\n   676â†’\n   677â†’```typescript\n   678â†’function haversineDistance(\n   679â†’  lat1: number, lon1: number,\n   680â†’  lat2: number, lon2: number\n   681â†’): number {\n   682â†’  const R = 6371;\n   683â†’  const toRad = (deg: number) => deg * Math.PI / 180;\n   684â†’\n   685â†’  const lat1Rad = toRad(lat1);\n   686â†’  const lat2Rad = toRad(lat2);\n   687â†’  const deltaLat = toRad(lat2 - lat1);\n   688â†’  const deltaLon = toRad(lon2 - lon1);\n   689â†’\n   690â†’  const a = Math.sin(deltaLat / 2) ** 2 +\n   691â†’            Math.cos(lat1Rad) * Math.cos(lat2Rad) *\n   692â†’            Math.sin(deltaLon / 2) ** 2;\n   693â†’\n   694â†’  const c = 2 * Math.atan2(Math.sqrt(a), Math.sqrt(1 - a));\n   695â†’\n   696â†’  return R * c;\n   697â†’}\n   698â†’```\n   699â†’\n   700â†’---\n   701â†’\n   702â†’## 5. Maintenance Level Mapping\n   703â†’\n   704â†’### Authoritative Mapping Table\n   705â†’\n   706â†’| Code | Railway Term | Display | Interval (km) | Tolerance | Duration | Man-Hours | Tracks | Cost |\n   707â†’|------|--------------|---------|---------------|-----------|----------|-----------|--------|------|\n   708â†’| A | PM1 | A-Check | 10,000 | +/- 2,000 | 4h | 8 | 1 | $2,500 |\n   709â†’| B | PM2 | B-Check | 50,000 | +/- 5,000 | 16h | 32 | 1 | $8,500 |\n   710â†’| C | PM3 | C-Check | 200,000 | +/- 20,000 | 72h | 160 | 1 | $45,000 |\n   711â†’| D | HM | D-Overhaul | 800,000 | +/- 50,000 | 480h | 1,200 | 2 | $180,000 |\n   712â†’\n   713â†’#### Terminology Key\n   714â†’\n   715â†’| Term | Meaning |\n   716â†’|------|---------|\n   717â†’| **PM1** | Preventive Maintenance Level 1 (Light) |\n   718â†’| **PM2** | Preventive Maintenance Level 2 (Intermediate) |\n   719â†’| **PM3** | Preventive Maintenance Level 3 (Scheduled) |\n   720â†’| **HM** | Heavy Maintenance / Major Overhaul |\n   721â†’| **OVH** | Full Overhaul / Refurbishment (resets all counters) |\n   722â†’\n   723â†’### Maintenance Cascade\n   724â†’\n   725â†’```\n   726â†’Fleet Type (e.g., Alstom Red Line)\n   727â†’ |\n   728â†’ +-- Maintenance Profile\n   729â†’     |\n   730â†’     +-- PM1 / A-Check (every 10,000 km)\n   731â†’     |   Duration: 4 hours\n   732â†’     |\n   733â†’     +-- PM2 / B-Check (every 50,000 km)\n   734â†’     |   Duration: 16 hours, INCLUDES PM1 tasks\n   735â†’     |\n   736â†’     +-- PM3 / C-Check (every 200,000 km)\n   737â†’     |   Duration: 72 hours, INCLUDES PM1 + PM2 tasks\n   738â†’     |\n   739â†’     +-- HM / D-Overhaul (every 800,000 km)\n   740â†’         Duration: 480 hours, RESETS counters\n   741â†’```\n   742â†’\n   743â†’### Maintenance Triggers\n   744â†’\n   745â†’| Trigger Type | Description | Best For |\n   746â†’|--------------|-------------|----------|\n   747â†’| Time-based | Calendar intervals | Simple assets, regulatory |\n   748â†’| Mileage-based | Odometer intervals (primary) | Wear-dependent components |\n   749â†’| Condition-based | Sensor thresholds | Critical components |\n   750â†’| Hybrid | Combined triggers | Most applications |\n   751â†’\n   752â†’---\n   753â†’\n   754â†’## 6. Domain Rules\n   755â†’\n   756â†’### 6.1 Hard Constraints (Never Violate)\n   757â†’\n   758â†’| ID | Rule | Rationale |\n   759â†’|----|------|-----------|\n   760â†’| HC-1 | Unfit trains NEVER assigned to service | Safety-critical |\n   761â†’| HC-2 | Maintenance windows are immutable | Planned downtime |\n   762â†’| HC-3 | Aptitude requirements are binary | Route compatibility |\n   763â†’| HC-4 | Depot capacity is physical | Track slots |\n   764â†’| HC-5 | Service cannot be cancelled for balancing | Operations > Optimization |\n   765â†’\n   766â†’### 6.2 Soft Constraints (Optimize Within)\n   767â†’\n   768â†’| ID | Rule | Weight |\n   769â†’|----|------|--------|\n   770â†’| SC-1 | Km balancing priority | 1000 (HIGH) |\n   771â†’| SC-2 | Dead running minimization | 100 (MEDIUM) |\n   772â†’| SC-3 | Maintenance depot proximity | 10 (LOW) |\n   773â†’\n   774â†’### 6.3 Tolerance Windows\n   775â†’\n   776â†’| Level | Tolerance | Overrun Warning | Critical |\n   777â†’|-------|-----------|-----------------|----------|\n   778â†’| PM1 / A-Check | +/- 2,000 km | 95% | 105% |\n   779â†’| PM2 / B-Check | +/- 5,000 km | 95% | 105% |\n   780â†’| PM3 / C-Check | +/- 20,000 km | 95% | 105% |\n   781â†’| HM / D-Overhaul | +/- 50,000 km | 95% | 105% |\n   782â†’\n   783â†’---\n   784â†’\n   785â†’## 7. Train State Machine\n   786â†’\n   787â†’```\n   788â†’    +-----------------------------------------------+\n   789â†’    |                                               |\n   790â†’    v                                               |\n   791â†’+-------+     +------------+     +------------+     |\n   792â†’|  FIT  |---->| IN-SERVICE |---->| AVAILABLE  |----+\n   793â†’+---+---+     +------------+     +-----+------+\n   794â†’    |                                  |\n   795â†’    |         +------------+           |\n   796â†’    +-------->|   UNFIT    |<----------+\n   797â†’              +-----+------+\n   798â†’                    |\n   799â†’                    v\n   800â†’              +------------+\n   801â†’              |MAINTENANCE |\n   802â†’              +-----+------+\n   803â†’                    |\n   804â†’                    v\n   805â†’              +------------+\n   806â†’              |    FIT     |\n   807â†’              +------------+\n   808â†’```\n   809â†’\n   810â†’| State | Definition | Transitions |\n   811â†’|-------|------------|-------------|\n   812â†’| **FIT** | Train operationally ready | IN-SERVICE, UNFIT |\n   813â†’| **IN-SERVICE** | Running revenue service | AVAILABLE |\n   814â†’| **AVAILABLE** | Fit but not assigned | IN-SERVICE, UNFIT |\n   815â†’| **UNFIT** | Requires maintenance | MAINTENANCE |\n   816â†’| **MAINTENANCE** | Undergoing maintenance | FIT |\n   817â†’\n   818â†’---\n   819â†’\n   820â†’## 8. Planning Hierarchy\n   821â†’\n   822â†’```\n   823â†’+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n   824â†’|                MASTER PLAN (Annual/Multi-Year)               |\n   825â†’|  - Resource allocation across contract period                |\n   826â†’|  - Major overhaul scheduling (HM/D-Check)                    |\n   827â†’+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n   828â†’|                     MONTHLY PLAN                             |\n   829â†’|  - Medium-scale repairs allocation (PM2/PM3)                 |\n   830â†’|  - Workload balancing across weeks                           |\n   831â†’+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n   832â†’|                      WEEKLY PLAN                             |\n   833â†’|  - Detailed work orders                                      |\n   834â†’|  - Staff rostering and track allocation                      |\n   835â†’+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n   836â†’|                      DAILY PLAN                              |\n   837â†’|  - Execution scheduling (PM1/A-Check)                        |\n   838â†’|  - Real-time adjustments                                     |\n   839â†’+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n   840â†’```\n   841â†’\n   842â†’### Time Zone Concept (Frozen Periods)\n   843â†’\n   844â†’| Horizon | Zone | Constraint | Allowed Changes |\n   845â†’|---------|------|------------|-----------------|\n   846â†’| T > 12 weeks | **Liquid** | Soft | Structure, Volume, Frequency |\n   847â†’| T-12 to T-7 | **Slushy** | Medium | Timing, Resource Allocation |\n   848â†’| T-7 to T-48h | **Frozen** | Hard | Emergency Only |\n   849â†’| T < 48h | **Execution** | Fixed | Break-fix only |\n   850â†’\n   851â†’### MMS LOD Mapping\n   852â†’\n   853â†’| Planning Level | MMS LOD | Zoom Range |\n   854â†’|----------------|---------|------------|\n   855â†’| Master Plan | MACRO | < 0.5x |\n   856â†’| Monthly Plan | MESO | 0.5x - 1.0x |\n   857â†’| Weekly Plan | MESO | 1.0x - 2.0x |\n   858â†’| Daily Plan | MICRO | > 2.0x |\n   859â†’\n   860â†’---\n   861â†’\n   862â†’# PART II: TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE\n   863â†’\n   864â†’## 9. System Overview\n   865â†’\n   866â†’### 9.1 Architecture Diagram\n   867â†’\n   868â†’```\n   869â†’+==============================================================================+\n   870â†’|                          MMS V1.0 GENESIS ARCHITECTURE                        |\n   871â†’+==============================================================================+\n   872â†’\n   873â†’+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n   874â†’|                              REACT APPLICATION                                |\n   875â†’|                         (Vite 5.x + React 18.3.1)                            |\n   876â†’+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n   877â†’|                                                                               |\n   878â†’|  +------------------+  +---------------------+  +------------------------+   |\n   879â†’|  |    App.tsx       |  |   canvasStore       |  |   RBushSpatialIndex    |   |\n   880â†’|  |    (Layout)      |<-|   (@zazcart/tools)  |<-|   (O(log n) queries)   |   |\n   881â†’|  +------------------+  +---------------------+  +------------------------+   |\n   882â†’|           |                      |                         |                  |\n   883â†’+-----------|----------------------|-------------------------|------------------+\n   884â†’            v                      v                         v\n   885â†’+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n   886â†’|                         LOD DECISION LAYER                                    |\n   887â†’|    +----------------+    +----------------+    +----------------+             |\n   888â†’|    |  zoom < 0.5    |    | 0.5 <= z < 2.0 |    |   zoom >= 2.0  |             |\n   889â†’|    |     MACRO      |    |      MESO      |    |     MICRO      |             |\n   890â†’|    +-------+--------+    +-------+--------+    +-------+--------+             |\n   891â†’+------------|---------------------|---------------------|----------------------+\n   892â†’             v                     v                     v\n   893â†’+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n   894â†’|                          RENDERING LAYERS                                     |\n   895â†’|  +------------------+  +---------------------+  +------------------------+   |\n   896â†’|  |   MacroLayer     |  |    MesoLayer        |  |    MicroLayer          |   |\n   897â†’|  | InstancedMesh    |  |  InstancedMesh      |  |  InstancedMesh         |   |\n   898â†’|  | (heatmap cells)  |  |  (task blocks)      |  |  + HTML labels         |   |\n   899â†’|  | Cells: 600       |  |  Pre-alloc: 100k    |  |  + Line2 curves        |   |\n   900â†’|  +------------------+  +---------------------+  +------------------------+   |\n   901â†’+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n   902â†’|                 REACT THREE FIBER 8.x + THREE.JS 0.170.x                     |\n   903â†’|                         OrthographicCamera                                    |\n   904â†’|                    X = Time (days) | Y = Train Index                         |\n   905â†’+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n   906â†’```\n   907â†’\n   908â†’### 9.2 Technology Stack\n   909â†’\n   910â†’| Layer | Technology | Version | Purpose |\n   911â†’|-------|------------|---------|---------|\n   912â†’| **Build Tool** | Vite | 5.x | Fast HMR, ESM-first |\n   913â†’| **UI Framework** | React | 18.3.1 | Required for BlueprintJS 6 |\n   914â†’| **UI Components** | BlueprintJS | 6.1.0 | Enterprise UI components |\n   915â†’| **3D Renderer** | React Three Fiber | 8.17.10 | Declarative Three.js |\n   916â†’| **3D Helpers** | drei | 9.114.3 | R3F ecosystem |\n   917â†’| **3D Engine** | Three.js | 0.170.0 | WebGL rendering |\n   918â†’| **State** | @zazcart/tools | 0.1.0 | Slice-based state machine |\n   919â†’| **Spatial Index** | RBush | 4.0.1 | R-tree for O(log n) queries |\n   920â†’| **Immutability** | Immer | 10.2.0 | Reducer state updates |\n   921â†’\n   922â†’### 9.3 Magna Rules\n   923â†’\n   924â†’These rules are INVIOLABLE for V1.0:\n   925â†’\n   926â†’| Rule | Constraint | Rationale |\n   927â†’|------|------------|-----------|\n   928â†’| **MAGNA-001** | React 18.3.1 (NOT 19) | BlueprintJS 6 requires React 18 |\n   929â†’| **MAGNA-002** | R3F 8.x + drei 9.x | Pairs with React 18 |\n   930â†’| **MAGNA-003** | 60fps minimum | Demo success criterion |\n   931â†’| **MAGNA-004** | Desktop-first | Mobile is deferred |\n   932â†’| **MAGNA-005** | Mock data only | No real mileage integration |\n   933â†’| **MAGNA-006** | @zazcart/tools for state | Avoid useState stale closures |\n   934â†’| **MAGNA-007** | RBush for all queries | O(log n) is mandatory |\n   935â†’\n   936â†’---\n   937â†’\n   938â†’## 10. State Management\n   939â†’\n   940â†’### 10.1 Library Overview\n   941â†’\n   942â†’**Package:** `@zazcart/tools` (located at `packages/toolkit`)\n   943â†’\n   944â†’The library provides a slice-based state machine pattern with:\n   945â†’- Immer-powered immutable updates\n   946â†’- React 18 `useSyncExternalStore` integration\n   947â†’- Zero-allocation option for 60fps updates\n   948â†’\n   949â†’**Note:** `@zazcart/tools` and `@belt/tools` are the SAME library.\n   950â†’\n   951â†’### 10.2 createMachine Pattern\n   952â†’\n   953â†’```typescript\n   954â†’// File: apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts\n   955â†’import { createMachine } from '@zazcart/tools';\n   956â†’import { viewportSlice } from './viewport';\n   957â†’import { interactionSlice } from './interaction';\n   958â†’\n   959â†’export const canvasMachine = createMachine({\n   960â†’  id: 'canvas',\n   961â†’  slices: {\n   962â†’    viewport: viewportSlice,\n   963â†’    interaction: interactionSlice,\n   964â†’  },\n   965â†’  context: {\n   966â†’    userID: null,\n   967â†’  },\n   968â†’  // CRITICAL: Disable metadata tracking for 60fps\n   969â†’  metadata: false,\n   970â†’});\n   971â†’\n   972â†’export const useCanvas = canvasMachine.useMachine;\n   973â†’export const useCanvasSelector = canvasMachine.useSelector;\n   974â†’export const canvasActions = canvasMachine.actions;\n   975â†’\n   976â†’export const canvasStore = {\n   977â†’  getState: canvasMachine.getState,\n   978â†’  dispatch: canvasMachine.dispatch,\n   979â†’  subscribe: canvasMachine.subscribe,\n   980â†’};\n   981â†’```\n   982â†’\n   983â†’### 10.3 Slice Definitions\n   984â†’\n   985â†’#### Viewport Slice\n   986â†’\n   987â†’```typescript\n   988â†’// File: apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts\n   989â†’import { createSyncSlice } from '@zazcart/tools';\n   990â†’\n   991â†’export interface CameraPosition {\n   992â†’  x: number;\n   993â†’  y: number;\n   994â†’  zoom: number;\n   995â†’}\n   996â†’\n   997â†’export interface ViewportSliceState {\n   998â†’  camera: CameraPosition;\n   999â†’  canvasSize: { width: number; height: number };\n  1000â†’  isInitialized: boolean;\n  1001â†’  momentum: { velocityX: number; velocityY: number; isActive: boolean };\n  1002â†’  animation: {\n  1003â†’    isAnimating: boolean;\n  1004â†’    startTime: number;\n  1005â†’    duration: number;\n  1006â†’    startX: number; startY: number; startZoom: number;\n  1007â†’    targetX: number; targetY: number; targetZoom: number;\n  1008â†’  };\n  1009â†’  isPanning: boolean;\n  1010â†’  panStart: CameraPosition;\n  1011â†’  cameraPosAtPanStart: CameraPosition;\n  1012â†’}\n  1013â†’\n  1014â†’export const viewportSlice = createSyncSlice({\n  1015â†’  id: 'viewport',\n  1016â†’  initial: {\n  1017â†’    camera: { x: 0, y: 0, zoom: 1 },\n  1018â†’    canvasSize: { width: 0, height: 0 },\n  1019â†’    isInitialized: false,\n  1020â†’    momentum: { velocityX: 0, velocityY: 0, isActive: false },\n  1021â†’    animation: {\n  1022â†’      isAnimating: false, startTime: 0, duration: 250,\n  1023â†’      startX: 0, startY: 0, startZoom: 1,\n  1024â†’      targetX: 0, targetY: 0, targetZoom: 1,\n  1025â†’    },\n  1026â†’    isPanning: false,\n  1027â†’    panStart: { x: 0, y: 0, zoom: 1 },\n  1028â†’    cameraPosAtPanStart: { x: 0, y: 0, zoom: 1 },\n  1029â†’  },\n  1030â†’  cache: { policy: 'session' },\n  1031â†’  reducers: {\n  1032â†’    setCamera(state, payload: CameraPosition) { state.camera = payload; },\n  1033â†’    setZoom(state, payload: number) { state.camera.zoom = payload; },\n  1034â†’    setCanvasSize(state, payload: { width: number; height: number }) {\n  1035â†’      state.canvasSize = payload;\n  1036â†’      state.isInitialized = true;\n  1037â†’    },\n  1038â†’    startPan(state, payload: { x: number; y: number }) {\n  1039â†’      state.isPanning = true;\n  1040â†’      state.panStart = { x: payload.x, y: payload.y, zoom: state.camera.zoom };\n  1041â†’      state.cameraPosAtPanStart = { ...state.camera };\n  1042â†’    },\n  1043â†’    endPan(state) { state.isPanning = false; },\n  1044â†’    startMomentum(state, payload: { velocityX: number; velocityY: number }) {\n  1045â†’      state.momentum = { ...payload, isActive: true };\n  1046â†’    },\n  1047â†’    stopMomentum(state) {\n  1048â†’      state.momentum = { velocityX: 0, velocityY: 0, isActive: false };\n  1049â†’    },\n  1050â†’    reset() { return viewportSlice.initial; },\n  1051â†’  },\n  1052â†’});\n  1053â†’```\n  1054â†’\n  1055â†’#### Interaction Slice\n  1056â†’\n  1057â†’```typescript\n  1058â†’// File: apps/canvas/src/state/interaction.ts\n  1059â†’import { createSyncSlice } from '@zazcart/tools';\n  1060â†’\n  1061â†’export interface InteractionSliceState {\n  1062â†’  hoveredTaskId: string | null;\n  1063â†’  selectedTaskId: string | null;\n  1064â†’  focusedTaskId: string | null;\n  1065â†’  mousePosition: { x: number; y: number } | null;\n  1066â†’}\n  1067â†’\n  1068â†’export const interactionSlice = createSyncSlice({\n  1069â†’  id: 'interaction',\n  1070â†’  initial: {\n  1071â†’    hoveredTaskId: null,\n  1072â†’    selectedTaskId: null,\n  1073â†’    focusedTaskId: null,\n  1074â†’    mousePosition: null,\n  1075â†’  },\n  1076â†’  cache: { policy: 'session' },\n  1077â†’  reducers: {\n  1078â†’    setHoveredTask(state, payload: string | null) { state.hoveredTaskId = payload; },\n  1079â†’    setSelectedTask(state, payload: string | null) { state.selectedTaskId = payload; },\n  1080â†’    setFocusedTask(state, payload: string | null) { state.focusedTaskId = payload; },\n  1081â†’    setMousePosition(state, payload: { x: number; y: number } | null) {\n  1082â†’      state.mousePosition = payload;\n  1083â†’    },\n  1084â†’    clearSelection(state) {\n  1085â†’      state.selectedTaskId = null;\n  1086â†’      state.focusedTaskId = null;\n  1087â†’    },\n  1088â†’  },\n  1089â†’});\n  1090â†’```\n  1091â†’\n  1092â†’### 10.4 Usage Patterns\n  1093â†’\n  1094â†’**Pattern 1: React Component with Selector**\n  1095â†’\n  1096â†’```typescript\n  1097â†’import { useCanvasSelector, canvasActions, canvasStore } from '../state';\n  1098â†’\n  1099â†’function ZoomControls() {\n  1100â†’  const zoom = useCanvasSelector(s => s.viewport.camera.zoom);\n  1101â†’\n  1102â†’  const handleZoomIn = () => {\n  1103â†’    canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.setZoom(zoom * 1.2));\n  1104â†’  };\n  1105â†’\n  1106â†’  return (\n  1107â†’    <button onClick={handleZoomIn}>+</button>\n  1108â†’  );\n  1109â†’}\n  1110â†’```\n  1111â†’\n  1112â†’**Pattern 2: Animation Loop (NO React Hooks)**\n  1113â†’\n  1114â†’```typescript\n  1115â†’import { useFrame } from '@react-three/fiber';\n  1116â†’import { canvasStore, canvasActions } from '../state';\n  1117â†’\n  1118â†’function CameraAnimator() {\n  1119â†’  useFrame(() => {\n  1120â†’    // CORRECT: Get fresh state every frame\n  1121â†’    const state = canvasStore.getState();\n  1122â†’    const { animation, camera } = state.viewport;\n  1123â†’\n  1124â†’    if (!animation.isAnimating) return;\n  1125â†’\n  1126â†’    const elapsed = Date.now() - animation.startTime;\n  1127â†’    const progress = Math.min(elapsed / animation.duration, 1);\n  1128â†’\n  1129â†’    // Update camera...\n  1130â†’  });\n  1131â†’\n  1132â†’  return null;\n  1133â†’}\n  1134â†’```\n  1135â†’\n  1136â†’**Pattern 3: Event Handler**\n  1137â†’\n  1138â†’```typescript\n  1139â†’const handleWheel = useCallback((event: WheelEvent) => {\n  1140â†’  event.preventDefault();\n  1141â†’  const state = canvasStore.getState();\n  1142â†’  const { camera } = state.viewport;\n  1143â†’\n  1144â†’  const zoomDelta = -event.deltaY * ZOOM_SENSITIVITY;\n  1145â†’  const newZoom = clamp(camera.zoom * (1 + zoomDelta), MIN_ZOOM, MAX_ZOOM);\n  1146â†’\n  1147â†’  canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.setCamera({\n  1148â†’    x: camera.x, y: camera.y, zoom: newZoom,\n  1149â†’  }));\n  1150â†’}, []);\n  1151â†’```\n  1152â†’\n  1153â†’---\n  1154â†’\n  1155â†’## 11. Canvas Architecture\n  1156â†’\n  1157â†’### 11.1 React Three Fiber Integration\n  1158â†’\n  1159â†’**Version Pairing (Critical):**\n  1160â†’\n  1161â†’| React Version | R3F Version | drei Version |\n  1162â†’|---------------|-------------|--------------|\n  1163â†’| **React 18** | **R3F 8.x** | **drei 9.x** |\n  1164â†’| React 19 | R3F 9.x | drei 10.x |\n  1165â†’\n  1166â†’```typescript\n  1167â†’// Camera configuration\n  1168â†’const CAMERA_NEAR = -1000;\n  1169â†’const CAMERA_FAR = 1000;\n  1170â†’const INITIAL_ZOOM = 1;\n  1171â†’\n  1172â†’<Canvas\n  1173â†’  orthographic\n  1174â†’  camera={{\n  1175â†’    position: [0, 0, 100],\n  1176â†’    zoom: INITIAL_ZOOM,\n  1177â†’    near: CAMERA_NEAR,\n  1178â†’    far: CAMERA_FAR,\n  1179â†’  }}\n  1180â†’  gl={{\n  1181â†’    antialias: true,\n  1182â†’    alpha: false,\n  1183â†’    powerPreference: 'high-performance',\n  1184â†’  }}\n  1185â†’>\n  1186â†’  <CameraController />\n  1187â†’  <LODLayers />\n  1188â†’</Canvas>\n  1189â†’```\n  1190â†’\n  1191â†’### 11.2 Coordinate System\n  1192â†’\n  1193â†’```\n  1194â†’                                    +X (Time - Days)\n  1195â†’                                    |\n  1196â†’        (-) Past                    |                    (+) Future\n  1197â†’   <--------------------------------+--------------------------------->\n  1198â†’                                    |\n  1199â†’                   Day 0 = meta.startDate (2024-01-01)\n  1200â†’\n  1201â†’+Y (Trains - negative because canvas Y grows down)\n  1202â†’|\n  1203â†’|  Train 0:  y = 0\n  1204â†’|  Train 1:  y = -24 (PX_PER_TRAIN)\n  1205â†’|  Train 2:  y = -48\n  1206â†’|  ...\n  1207â†’|  Train 49: y = -1176\n  1208â†’v\n  1209â†’\n  1210â†’Constants:\n  1211â†’  PX_PER_DAY = 10\n  1212â†’  PX_PER_TRAIN = 24\n  1213â†’\n  1214â†’Viewport:\n  1215â†’  Camera position (x, y) is CENTER of visible area\n  1216â†’  Visible width = canvasWidth / zoom\n  1217â†’  Visible height = canvasHeight / zoom\n  1218â†’```\n  1219â†’\n  1220â†’---\n  1221â†’\n  1222â†’## 12. LOD System\n  1223â†’\n  1224â†’### 12.1 Three-Layer Architecture\n  1225â†’\n  1226â†’| Layer | Zoom Range | Visualization | Max Instances |\n  1227â†’|-------|------------|---------------|---------------|\n  1228â†’| **MACRO** | < 0.5x | Heatmap cells | 600 |\n  1229â†’| **MESO** | 0.5x - 2.0x | Task blocks | 100,000 |\n  1230â†’| **MICRO** | > 2.0x | HTML labels + curves | 50 labels |\n  1231â†’\n  1232â†’### 12.2 Crossfade Transitions\n  1233â†’\n  1234â†’```typescript\n  1235â†’const LOD_THRESHOLDS = {\n  1236â†’  macroToMeso: 0.5,\n  1237â†’  mesoToMicro: 2.0,\n  1238â†’};\n  1239â†’const BLEND_ZONE_SIZE = 0.15;\n  1240â†’\n  1241â†’function calculateLayerOpacities(zoom: number): {\n  1242â†’  macro: number; meso: number; micro: number;\n  1243â†’} {\n  1244â†’  const { macroToMeso, mesoToMicro } = LOD_THRESHOLDS;\n  1245â†’\n  1246â†’  const macroEnd = macroToMeso - BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;\n  1247â†’  const mesoStart = macroToMeso + BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;\n  1248â†’  const mesoEnd = mesoToMicro - BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;\n  1249â†’  const microStart = mesoToMicro + BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;\n  1250â†’\n  1251â†’  let macro = 0, meso = 0, micro = 0;\n  1252â†’\n  1253â†’  if (zoom <= macroEnd) macro = 1;\n  1254â†’  else if (zoom < mesoStart) {\n  1255â†’    const t = (zoom - macroEnd) / (mesoStart - macroEnd);\n  1256â†’    macro = 1 - t;\n  1257â†’    meso = t;\n  1258â†’  } else if (zoom <= mesoEnd) {\n  1259â†’    meso = 1;\n  1260â†’  } else if (zoom < microStart) {\n  1261â†’    const t = (zoom - mesoEnd) / (microStart - mesoEnd);\n  1262â†’    meso = 1 - t;\n  1263â†’    micro = t;\n  1264â†’  } else {\n  1265â†’    micro = 1;\n  1266â†’  }\n  1267â†’\n  1268â†’  return { macro, meso, micro };\n  1269â†’}\n  1270â†’```\n  1271â†’\n  1272â†’**Material Configuration:**\n  1273â†’\n  1274â†’```typescript\n  1275â†’<meshBasicMaterial\n  1276â†’  toneMapped={false}\n  1277â†’  transparent={opacity < 1}\n  1278â†’  opacity={opacity}\n  1279â†’  depthWrite={opacity >= 0.5}  // Prevent z-fighting\n  1280â†’/>\n  1281â†’```\n  1282â†’\n  1283â†’### 12.3 Layer Implementations\n  1284â†’\n  1285â†’#### MacroLayer\n  1286â†’\n  1287â†’```typescript\n  1288â†’// InstancedMesh with heatmap coloring\n  1289â†’const CELL_WIDTH = 30 * 10;  // ~30 days * pxPerDay\n  1290â†’const CELL_HEIGHT = 24;\n  1291â†’\n  1292â†’export function MacroLayer({ tasks, trainCount, opacity = 1 }) {\n  1293â†’  const meshRef = useRef<InstancedMesh>(null);\n  1294â†’  const tempObject = useMemo(() => new Object3D(), []);\n  1295â†’  const tempColor = useMemo(() => new Color(), []);\n  1296â†’\n  1297â†’  const totalMonths = 10 * 12;\n  1298â†’  const totalCells = trainCount * totalMonths;\n  1299â†’\n  1300â†’  const densityGrid = useMemo(\n  1301â†’    () => calculateDensityGrid(tasks, trainCount, totalMonths),\n  1302â†’    [tasks, trainCount, totalMonths]\n  1303â†’  );\n  1304â†’\n  1305â†’  useEffect(() => {\n  1306â†’    if (!meshRef.current) return;\n  1307â†’    // Update instance matrices and colors based on density...\n  1308â†’  }, [densityGrid]);\n  1309â†’\n  1310â†’  return (\n  1311â†’    <instancedMesh ref={meshRef} args={[undefined, undefined, totalCells]}>\n  1312â†’      <planeGeometry args={[1, 1]} />\n  1313â†’      <meshBasicMaterial toneMapped={false} transparent={opacity < 1}\n  1314â†’        opacity={opacity} depthWrite={opacity >= 0.5} vertexColors />\n  1315â†’    </instancedMesh>\n  1316â†’  );\n  1317â†’}\n  1318â†’```\n  1319â†’\n  1320â†’#### MesoLayer\n  1321â†’\n  1322â†’```typescript\n  1323â†’const MAX_INSTANCES = 100000;\n  1324â†’const TASK_HEIGHT = 18;\n  1325â†’\n  1326â†’const COLORS = {\n  1327â†’  blue: '#3b82f6',\n  1328â†’  orange: '#f59e0b',\n  1329â†’  gray: '#9ca3af',\n  1330â†’};\n  1331â†’const CONFLICT_COLOR = '#ef4444';\n  1332â†’\n  1333â†’export function MesoLayer({ tasks, opacity = 1 }) {\n  1334â†’  const meshRef = useRef<InstancedMesh>(null);\n  1335â†’  const tempObject = useMemo(() => new Object3D(), []);\n  1336â†’  const tempColor = useMemo(() => new Color(), []);\n  1337â†’\n  1338â†’  useEffect(() => {\n  1339â†’    if (!meshRef.current) return;\n  1340â†’    meshRef.current.count = tasks.length;\n  1341â†’\n  1342â†’    tasks.forEach((task, i) => {\n  1343â†’      const width = task.duration * PX_PER_DAY;\n  1344â†’      const x = task.startDay * PX_PER_DAY + width / 2;\n  1345â†’      const y = -task.trainIndex * PX_PER_TRAIN - PX_PER_TRAIN / 2;\n  1346â†’\n  1347â†’      tempObject.position.set(x, y, 0);\n  1348â†’      tempObject.scale.set(width, TASK_HEIGHT, 1);\n  1349â†’      tempObject.updateMatrix();\n  1350â†’      meshRef.current.setMatrixAt(i, tempObject.matrix);\n  1351â†’\n  1352â†’      const color = task.hasConflict ? CONFLICT_COLOR : COLORS[task.color];\n  1353â†’      tempColor.set(color);\n  1354â†’      meshRef.current.setColorAt(i, tempColor);\n  1355â†’    });\n  1356â†’\n  1357â†’    meshRef.current.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate = true;\n  1358â†’    if (meshRef.current.instanceColor) {\n  1359â†’      meshRef.current.instanceColor.needsUpdate = true;\n  1360â†’    }\n  1361â†’  }, [tasks]);\n  1362â†’\n  1363â†’  return (\n  1364â†’    <instancedMesh ref={meshRef} args={[undefined, undefined, MAX_INSTANCES]}>\n  1365â†’      <planeGeometry args={[1, 1]} />\n  1366â†’      <meshBasicMaterial toneMapped={false} transparent={opacity < 1}\n  1367â†’        opacity={opacity} depthWrite={opacity >= 0.5} vertexColors />\n  1368â†’    </instancedMesh>\n  1369â†’  );\n  1370â†’}\n  1371â†’```\n  1372â†’\n  1373â†’#### MicroLayer\n  1374â†’\n  1375â†’```typescript\n  1376â†’const MAX_VISIBLE_LABELS = 50;\n  1377â†’\n  1378â†’export function MicroLayer({ tasks, viewportBounds, opacity = 1 }) {\n  1379â†’  const visibleTasks = useMemo(() => {\n  1380â†’    if (!viewportBounds) return tasks.slice(0, MAX_VISIBLE_LABELS);\n  1381â†’    const visible = spatialIndex.queryViewport(viewportBounds);\n  1382â†’    return visible.slice(0, MAX_VISIBLE_LABELS);\n  1383â†’  }, [tasks, viewportBounds]);\n  1384â†’\n  1385â†’  return (\n  1386â†’    <group>\n  1387â†’      <TaskBlocks tasks={tasks} opacity={opacity} />\n  1388â†’      {visibleTasks.map(task => (\n  1389â†’        <TaskLabel key={task.id} task={task} opacity={opacity} />\n  1390â†’      ))}\n  1391â†’      <DependencyLines tasks={visibleTasks} opacity={opacity} />\n  1392â†’    </group>\n  1393â†’  );\n  1394â†’}\n  1395â†’```\n  1396â†’\n  1397â†’---\n  1398â†’\n  1399â†’## 13. Performance Architecture\n  1400â†’\n  1401â†’### 13.1 Performance Requirements\n  1402â†’\n  1403â†’| Metric | Target | Confidence |\n  1404â†’|--------|--------|------------|\n  1405â†’| **Frame Rate** | 60fps constant | 70% (desktop) |\n  1406â†’| **Frame Budget** | < 16.67ms | 70% (desktop) |\n  1407â†’| **Viewport Query** | < 1ms | 95% (verified) |\n  1408â†’| **LOD Transition** | < 100ms | 90% |\n  1409â†’| **Memory** | < 100MB stable | 80% |\n  1410â†’\n  1411â†’### 13.2 InstancedMesh Pre-allocation\n  1412â†’\n  1413â†’**Problem:** Dynamic count causes GPU buffer reallocation.\n  1414â†’\n  1415â†’```typescript\n  1416â†’// WRONG: Recreates buffer when length changes\n  1417â†’<instancedMesh args={[geometry, material, tasks.length]}>\n  1418â†’\n  1419â†’// CORRECT: Pre-allocate, control with mesh.count\n  1420â†’const MAX_INSTANCES = 100000;\n  1421â†’<instancedMesh ref={meshRef} args={[geometry, material, MAX_INSTANCES]}>\n  1422â†’\n  1423â†’// In useEffect:\n  1424â†’mesh.count = actualTaskCount;\n  1425â†’```\n  1426â†’\n  1427â†’**Memory Cost:** 100k instances x 64 bytes = ~6.4MB GPU memory\n  1428â†’\n  1429â†’### 13.3 RBush Spatial Indexing\n  1430â†’\n  1431â†’**MANDATORY: All viewport queries must use RBush. Array.filter() is FORBIDDEN.**\n  1432â†’\n  1433â†’```typescript\n  1434â†’import RBush from 'rbush';\n  1435â†’\n  1436â†’interface RBushItem {\n  1437â†’  minX: number; minY: number;\n  1438â†’  maxX: number; maxY: number;\n  1439â†’  id: string;\n  1440â†’}\n  1441â†’\n  1442â†’export class RBushSpatialIndex {\n  1443â†’  private tree: RBush<RBushItem>;\n  1444â†’  private itemsById: Map<string, RBushItem> = new Map();\n  1445â†’\n  1446â†’  constructor() { this.tree = new RBush(); }\n  1447â†’\n  1448â†’  load(tasks: TaskRender[]): void {\n  1449â†’    this.tree.clear();\n  1450â†’    this.itemsById.clear();\n  1451â†’\n  1452â†’    const items: RBushItem[] = tasks.map(task => ({\n  1453â†’      minX: task.startDay * PX_PER_DAY,\n  1454â†’      maxX: (task.startDay + task.duration) * PX_PER_DAY,\n  1455â†’      minY: -task.trainIndex * PX_PER_TRAIN - PX_PER_TRAIN,\n  1456â†’      maxY: -task.trainIndex * PX_PER_TRAIN,\n  1457â†’      id: task.id,\n  1458â†’    }));\n  1459â†’\n  1460â†’    for (const item of items) {\n  1461â†’      this.itemsById.set(item.id, item);\n  1462â†’    }\n  1463â†’\n  1464â†’    this.tree.load(items);  // Bulk load O(n log n)\n  1465â†’  }\n  1466â†’\n  1467â†’  queryViewport(bounds: ViewportBounds): string[] {\n  1468â†’    const OVERSCAN = 100;\n  1469â†’    const results = this.tree.search({\n  1470â†’      minX: bounds.minX - OVERSCAN,\n  1471â†’      maxX: bounds.maxX + OVERSCAN,\n  1472â†’      minY: bounds.minY - OVERSCAN,\n  1473â†’      maxY: bounds.maxY + OVERSCAN,\n  1474â†’    });\n  1475â†’    return results.map(item => item.id);\n  1476â†’  }\n  1477â†’\n  1478â†’  queryNearest(x: number, y: number, maxDistance = 50): { id: string; distance: number } | null {\n  1479â†’    let radius = 20;\n  1480â†’    let nearest: { id: string; distance: number } | null = null;\n  1481â†’\n  1482â†’    while (radius <= maxDistance) {\n  1483â†’      const candidates = this.tree.search({\n  1484â†’        minX: x - radius, maxX: x + radius,\n  1485â†’        minY: y - radius, maxY: y + radius,\n  1486â†’      });\n  1487â†’\n  1488â†’      for (const item of candidates) {\n  1489â†’        const centerX = (item.minX + item.maxX) / 2;\n  1490â†’        const centerY = (item.minY + item.maxY) / 2;\n  1491â†’        const dist = Math.sqrt((x - centerX) ** 2 + (y - centerY) ** 2);\n  1492â†’\n  1493â†’        if (dist <= maxDistance && (!nearest || dist < nearest.distance)) {\n  1494â†’          nearest = { id: item.id, distance: dist };\n  1495â†’        }\n  1496â†’      }\n  1497â†’\n  1498â†’      if (nearest) return nearest;\n  1499â†’      radius *= 2;\n  1500â†’    }\n  1501â†’\n  1502â†’    return nearest;\n  1503â†’  }\n  1504â†’}\n  1505â†’```\n  1506â†’\n  1507â†’### 13.4 Object Pooling\n  1508â†’\n  1509â†’**MANDATORY: GC is the primary bottleneck in WebGL applications.**\n  1510â†’\n  1511â†’```typescript\n  1512â†’// In component scope (created once, reused every frame)\n  1513â†’const tempObject = useMemo(() => new Object3D(), []);\n  1514â†’const tempColor = useMemo(() => new Color(), []);\n  1515â†’const tempVector = useMemo(() => new Vector3(), []);\n  1516â†’\n  1517â†’// In animation loop\n  1518â†’useFrame(() => {\n  1519â†’  // CORRECT: Reuse pooled objects\n  1520â†’  tempVector.set(x, y, z);\n  1521â†’  ref.current.position.copy(tempVector);\n  1522â†’\n  1523â†’  // WRONG: Creates new object every frame\n  1524â†’  // ref.current.position.copy(new Vector3(x, y, z));\n  1525â†’});\n  1526â†’```\n  1527â†’\n  1528â†’### 13.5 GC Avoidance\n  1529â†’\n  1530â†’| Strategy | Implementation | Impact |\n  1531â†’|----------|----------------|--------|\n  1532â†’| Object Pooling | useMemo for temp objects | High |\n  1533â†’| metadata: false | State machine config | Medium |\n  1534â†’| TypedArrays | EntityStore SoA pattern | High |\n  1535â†’| Pre-allocation | InstancedMesh MAX_INSTANCES | High |\n  1536â†’| Bulk operations | RBush.load() | Medium |\n  1537â†’\n  1538â†’**Frame Budget: 16.67ms**\n  1539â†’\n  1540â†’| Operation | Budget | Priority |\n  1541â†’|-----------|--------|----------|\n  1542â†’| GPU Render | ~8ms | Critical |\n  1543â†’| JavaScript | ~5ms | Critical |\n  1544â†’| Layout/Paint | ~2ms | Low |\n  1545â†’| GC | **0ms target** | **Eliminate** |\n  1546â†’\n  1547â†’---\n  1548â†’\n  1549â†’## 14. Data Flow\n  1550â†’\n  1551â†’### Data Generation Pipeline\n  1552â†’\n  1553â†’```\n  1554â†’+-------------------------------------------------------------------+\n  1555â†’|                      DATA GENERATION PIPELINE                      |\n  1556â†’+-------------------------------------------------------------------+\n  1557â†’\n  1558â†’+------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+\n  1559â†’|   Generator      | --> |   State Store    | --> |   Render Layer   |\n  1560â†’|   (mock-data.ts) |     |   (canvasStore)  |     |   (LOD Layers)   |\n  1561â†’+--------+---------+     +--------+---------+     +--------+---------+\n  1562â†’         |                        |                        |\n  1563â†’         v                        v                        v\n  1564â†’+------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+\n  1565â†’| Persistent Types |     | Runtime State    |     | GPU Buffers      |\n  1566â†’| - Train          |     | - ViewportState  |     | - InstanceMatrix |\n  1567â†’| - Task           |     | - Interaction    |     | - InstanceColor  |\n  1568â†’| - DatasetMeta    |     | - EntityStore    |     | - HTML overlays  |\n  1569â†’+------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+\n  1570â†’```\n  1571â†’\n  1572â†’### Viewport Culling Flow\n  1573â†’\n  1574â†’```\n  1575â†’1. User pans/zooms canvas\n  1576â†’        |\n  1577â†’        v\n  1578â†’2. Camera position updates in state\n  1579â†’        |\n  1580â†’        v\n  1581â†’3. Calculate viewport bounds in world coordinates\n  1582â†’        |\n  1583â†’        v\n  1584â†’4. Query RBush spatial index (O(log n + k))\n  1585â†’        |\n  1586â†’        v\n  1587â†’5. Map IDs to TaskRender objects\n  1588â†’        |\n  1589â†’        v\n  1590â†’6. Update InstancedMesh:\n  1591â†’   mesh.count = visibleTasks.length\n  1592â†’   for each task: setMatrixAt, setColorAt\n  1593â†’   mesh.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate = true\n  1594â†’```\n  1595â†’\n  1596â†’---\n  1597â†’\n  1598â†’# PART III: USER EXPERIENCE\n  1599â†’\n  1600â†’## 15. Semantic Zoom Concept\n  1601â†’\n  1602â†’### Core Metaphor\n  1603â†’\n  1604â†’**\"Google Earth for Time\"** - The visualization transforms based on zoom level.\n  1605â†’\n  1606â†’```\n  1607â†’   ZOOM OUT (Macro)              ZOOM LEVEL               ZOOM IN (Micro)\n  1608â†’        <---------------------------------------------------------------->\n  1609â†’\n  1610â†’   +-------------+          +-------------+          +-------------+\n  1611â†’   | â–‘â–‘â–“â–“â–‘â–‘â–‘â–‘â–‘â–‘ |          | â–“â–“â–“ â–‘â–‘â–‘ â–“â–“ |          | +--------+ |\n  1612â†’   | â–‘â–‘â–‘â–“â–“â–“â–‘â–‘â–‘â–‘ |          | â–‘â–‘ â–“â–“â–“ â–‘â–‘â–‘ |          | | Task A | |\n  1613â†’   | â–‘â–“â–“â–“â–‘â–‘â–‘â–‘â–‘â–‘ |          | â–“â–“ â–‘â–‘â–‘ â–“â–“â–“ |          | | 8h     | |\n  1614â†’   +-------------+          +-------------+          +-------------+\n  1615â†’\n  1616â†’   12-YEAR HEATMAP          2-YEAR DNA STRIP        4-WEEK GANTT\n  1617â†’   (Strategic)               (Tactical)              (Operational)\n  1618â†’```\n  1619â†’\n  1620â†’| Zoom Level | Data Representation | User Question |\n  1621â†’|------------|---------------------|---------------|\n  1622â†’| **MACRO** | Aggregated density | \"Where are bottlenecks?\" |\n  1623â†’| **MESO** | Task blocks | \"What's the maintenance rhythm?\" |\n  1624â†’| **MICRO** | Detailed cards | \"What's this week's work?\" |\n  1625â†’\n  1626â†’### Continuous Navigation\n  1627â†’\n  1628â†’| Gesture | Action | Effect |\n  1629â†’|---------|--------|--------|\n  1630â†’| **Drag** | Pan canvas | Navigate through time and trains |\n  1631â†’| **Scroll wheel** | Zoom at cursor | Increase/decrease detail |\n  1632â†’| **Pinch** | Zoom at center | Two-finger zoom |\n  1633â†’| **Double-click** | Zoom in 2x | Quick zoom to point |\n  1634â†’\n  1635â†’---\n  1636â†’\n  1637â†’## 16. Visual Design System\n  1638â†’\n  1639â†’### 16.1 Color Palette\n  1640â†’\n  1641â†’**Primary Colors:**\n  1642â†’\n  1643â†’| Usage | Color | Hex |\n  1644â†’|-------|-------|-----|\n  1645â†’| Background | Zinc 100 | `#F4F4F5` |\n  1646â†’| Surface | White | `#FFFFFF` |\n  1647â†’| Text Primary | Gray 900 | `#111827` |\n  1648â†’| Text Secondary | Gray 500 | `#6B7280` |\n  1649â†’\n  1650â†’**Task Type Colors:**\n  1651â†’\n  1652â†’| Type | Color | Hex |\n  1653â†’|------|-------|-----|\n  1654â†’| Maintenance | Blue 700 | `#1D4ED8` |\n  1655â†’| Overhaul | Amber 500 | `#F59E0B` |\n  1656â†’| Inspection | Gray 500 | `#6B7280` |\n  1657â†’\n  1658â†’**Status Colors:**\n  1659â†’\n  1660â†’| Status | Color | Hex |\n  1661â†’|--------|-------|-----|\n  1662â†’| Conflict | Red 500 | `#EF4444` |\n  1663â†’| Selected | Cyan 500 | `#06B6D4` |\n  1664â†’| Focused | Violet 500 | `#8B5CF6` |\n  1665â†’| Success | Green 500 | `#22C55E` |\n  1666â†’| Warning | Yellow 500 | `#EAB308` |\n  1667â†’\n  1668â†’**Heatmap Gradient:**\n  1669â†’\n  1670â†’```\n  1671â†’Low Density ---------------------------------> High Density\n  1672â†’\n  1673â†’   #FFF3C4      #FDE68A      #FB923C      #EA580C      #DC2626\n  1674â†’   (Yellow)    (Lt Orange)  (Orange)    (Dp Orange)   (Red)\n  1675â†’      |            |            |            |            |\n  1676â†’   0-20%       20-40%       40-60%       60-80%       80-100%\n  1677â†’```\n  1678â†’\n  1679â†’### 16.2 Typography\n  1680â†’\n  1681â†’| Element | Font | Size | Weight |\n  1682â†’|---------|------|------|--------|\n  1683â†’| App Title | System sans-serif | 18px | Bold (700) |\n  1684â†’| Section Headers | System sans-serif | 14px | Semibold (600) |\n  1685â†’| Task Labels | System sans-serif | 11px | Regular (400) |\n  1686â†’| Timeline Dates | System sans-serif | 12px | Semibold (600) |\n  1687â†’| Train IDs | System monospace | 12px | Regular (400) |\n  1688â†’| Tooltips | System sans-serif | 13px | Regular (400) |\n  1689â†’\n  1690â†’---\n  1691â†’\n  1692â†’## 17. Layout Structure\n  1693â†’\n  1694â†’```\n  1695â†’+--------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n  1696â†’|                               NAVBAR                                      |\n  1697â†’|  +----------------------------------------------------------------------+|\n  1698â†’|  | Metro Integrated Planning System    | Fleet: 98% | [Zoom: 1.00x]    ||\n  1699â†’|  +----------------------------------------------------------------------+|\n  1700â†’+----------+---------------------------------------------------------------+\n  1701â†’|          |                    TIMELINE HEADER                             |\n  1702â†’|          |  +----------------------------------------------------------+  |\n  1703â†’|  SIDEBAR |  | 2024    2025    2026    2027    2028    2029    2030    |  |\n  1704â†’|          |  +----------------------------------------------------------+  |\n  1705â†’| +------+ +---------------------------------------------------------------+\n  1706â†’| | T001 | |                                                               |\n  1707â†’| | T002 | |                     CANVAS AREA                               |\n  1708â†’| | T003 | |                                                               |\n  1709â†’| | T004 | |          (React Three Fiber + InstancedMesh)                 |\n  1710â†’| | T005 | |                                                    +---+      |\n  1711â†’| | ...  | |                                                    | + | Zoom |\n  1712â†’| | T050 | |   +--------+                                       +---+      |\n  1713â†’| +------+ |   |MINIMAP |                                       | - |      |\n  1714â†’|          |   +--------+                                       +---+      |\n  1715â†’+----------+---------------------------------------------------------------+\n  1716â†’|                              TIME SCRUBBER                                |\n  1717â†’|  +----------------------------------------------------------------------+|\n  1718â†’|  | [=====================================O=============================] ||\n  1719â†’|  +----------------------------------------------------------------------+|\n  1720â†’+--------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n  1721â†’```\n  1722â†’\n  1723â†’### Component Dimensions\n  1724â†’\n  1725â†’| Component | Width | Height |\n  1726â†’|-----------|-------|--------|\n  1727â†’| Navbar | 100% | 48px |\n  1728â†’| Sidebar | 80px | calc(100vh - 96px) |\n  1729â†’| Timeline | calc(100% - 80px) | 32px |\n  1730â†’| Canvas | calc(100% - 80px) | Remaining |\n  1731â†’| Minimap | 120px | 80px |\n  1732â†’| Zoom Controls | 40px | 80px |\n  1733â†’| Time Scrubber | 100% | 48px |\n  1734â†’\n  1735â†’---\n  1736â†’\n  1737â†’## 18. Interaction Patterns\n  1738â†’\n  1739â†’### 18.1 Pan Navigation\n  1740â†’\n  1741â†’```\n  1742â†’User drags from A to B\n  1743â†’\n  1744â†’        A ----------------> B\n  1745â†’\n  1746â†’Canvas moves in OPPOSITE direction\n  1747â†’(content appears to move with drag)\n  1748â†’```\n  1749â†’\n  1750â†’**Constraints:**\n  1751â†’- Unconstrained within data bounds\n  1752â†’- Soft-stop at edges with 10% overscan\n  1753â†’- Snap-back animation if dragged beyond\n  1754â†’\n  1755â†’### 18.2 Zoom Interaction\n  1756â†’\n  1757â†’```\n  1758â†’Scroll UP (away from user)    ->    Zoom IN (more detail)\n  1759â†’Scroll DOWN (toward user)     ->    Zoom OUT (less detail)\n  1760â†’\n  1761â†’Zoom anchored at cursor position\n  1762â†’```\n  1763â†’\n  1764â†’**Zoom Range:**\n  1765â†’\n  1766â†’| Constant | Value | Purpose |\n  1767â†’|----------|-------|---------|\n  1768â†’| MIN_ZOOM | 0.1 | 20 years visible |\n  1769â†’| MAX_ZOOM | 5.0 | 1 week visible |\n  1770â†’| DEFAULT_ZOOM | 1.0 | 5 years visible |\n  1771â†’\n  1772â†’### 18.3 Gesture Mapping\n  1773â†’\n  1774â†’| Gesture | Device | Action |\n  1775â†’|---------|--------|--------|\n  1776â†’| Left-drag | Mouse | Pan canvas |\n  1777â†’| Scroll wheel | Mouse | Zoom at cursor |\n  1778â†’| Two-finger scroll | Trackpad | Pan canvas |\n  1779â†’| Pinch | Trackpad/Touch | Zoom at center |\n  1780â†’| Ctrl + Scroll | All | Horizontal pan |\n  1781â†’| Shift + Scroll | All | Vertical pan |\n  1782â†’| Double-click | All | Zoom in 2x |\n  1783â†’\n  1784â†’### 18.4 Momentum Physics\n  1785â†’\n  1786â†’```typescript\n  1787â†’const FRICTION = 0.93;\n  1788â†’const MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5;\n  1789â†’\n  1790â†’function startMomentum(initialVelocity: { x: number; y: number }) {\n  1791â†’  let velocity = { ...initialVelocity };\n  1792â†’\n  1793â†’  function animate() {\n  1794â†’    velocity.x *= FRICTION;\n  1795â†’    velocity.y *= FRICTION;\n  1796â†’\n  1797â†’    pan(velocity.x, velocity.y);\n  1798â†’\n  1799â†’    const magnitude = Math.sqrt(velocity.x ** 2 + velocity.y ** 2);\n  1800â†’    if (magnitude > MIN_VELOCITY) {\n  1801â†’      requestAnimationFrame(animate);\n  1802â†’    }\n  1803â†’  }\n  1804â†’\n  1805â†’  requestAnimationFrame(animate);\n  1806â†’}\n  1807â†’```\n  1808â†’\n  1809â†’---\n  1810â†’\n  1811â†’## 19. Task Interaction States\n  1812â†’\n  1813â†’| State | Visual | Trigger | Exit |\n  1814â†’|-------|--------|---------|------|\n  1815â†’| **Normal** | Base color | Default | Any other state |\n  1816â†’| **Hover** | Brightness +30% | Mouse over | Mouse leave |\n  1817â†’| **Selected** | Cyan border (2px) | Click | Click elsewhere / Escape |\n  1818â†’| **Focused** | Violet ring (3px) | Tab navigation | Tab away |\n  1819â†’| **Conflict** | Red background | hasConflict: true | Data change |\n  1820â†’\n  1821â†’### Selection Flow\n  1822â†’\n  1823â†’```\n  1824â†’         click(task)\n  1825â†’              |\n  1826â†’              v\n  1827â†’       +------------+     click(other) / Escape\n  1828â†’       |  SELECTED  |------------------------+\n  1829â†’       +-----+------+                        |\n  1830â†’             |                               |\n  1831â†’             | click(task)                   |\n  1832â†’             v                               |\n  1833â†’       +------------+                        |\n  1834â†’       |  DETAILS   |------------------------+\n  1835â†’       |   PANEL    |\n  1836â†’       +------------+\n  1837â†’```\n  1838â†’\n  1839â†’---\n  1840â†’\n  1841â†’## 20. Keyboard Navigation\n  1842â†’\n  1843â†’| Key | Action |\n  1844â†’|-----|--------|\n  1845â†’| Tab | Focus next task |\n  1846â†’| Shift + Tab | Focus previous task |\n  1847â†’| Enter / Space | Select focused task |\n  1848â†’| Arrow Up/Down/Left/Right | Pan viewport |\n  1849â†’| +/- | Zoom in/out |\n  1850â†’| Escape | Clear selection / Close panel |\n  1851â†’| Home | Reset view to default |\n  1852â†’| End | Go to end of timeline |\n  1853â†’| Page Up/Down | Jump 1 year back/forward |\n  1854â†’\n  1855â†’---\n  1856â†’\n  1857â†’## 21. Accessibility\n  1858â†’\n  1859â†’### ARIA Implementation\n  1860â†’\n  1861â†’```tsx\n  1862â†’<div\n  1863â†’  role=\"application\"\n  1864â†’  aria-label=\"Maintenance planning canvas. 1975â†’```\n  1976â†’\n  1977â†’### Timeline Header\n  1978â†’\n  1979â†’**Adaptive Label Format:**\n  1980â†’\n  1981â†’| LOD Level | Visible Range | Label Format |\n Conflict Visualization\n  1990â†’\n  1991â†’### Conflict Types (V1.0)\n  1992â†’\n  1993â†’| Type | Visual | Description |\n  1994â†’|------|--------|-------------|\n  1995â†’| Task Overlap | Red background | Two tasks on same train at same time |\n  1996â†’\n  1997â†’### Conflict Indication\n  1998â†’\n  1999â†’**MESO Layer:**\n  2000â†’- Normal task: Blue fill\n  2001â†’- Conflict task: Red fill + thicker border\n  2002â†’\n  2003â†’**MICRO Layer:**\n  2004â†’- Red border on task card\n  2005â†’- Warning icon in top-right\n  2006â†’\n  2007â†’### Conflict Colors\n  2008â†’\n  2009â†’```typescript\n  2010â†’const CONFLICT_COLORS = {\n  2011â†’  background: '#FEE2E2',  // Red 100\n  2012â†’  border: '#EF4444',      // Red 500\n  2013â†’  icon: '#DC2626',        // Red 600\n  2014â†’  text: '#991B1B',        // Red 800\n  2015â†’};\n  2016â†’```\n  2017â†’\n  2018â†’---\n  2019â†’\n  2020â†’## 24.
