APPS/CANVAS - CURRENT STATE\n\n### 2.1 Core Architecture\n\n**Tech Stack:**\n- **React 18.3.1** + **Vite 5.x** (build/dev)\n- **React Three Fiber 8.17.10** + **Three.js 0.170.0** (WebGL)\n- **drei 9.114.3** (R3F helpers: Html, Line, etc.)\n- **BlueprintJS 6.1.0** (UI components: Navbar, Card, Button)\n- **@zazcart/tools 0.1.0** (State machine library)\n- **rbush 4.0.1** (R-tree spatial indexing)\n- **D3 modules** (d3-array, d3-scale, d3-time, d3-scale-chromatic, d3-zoom, etc.)\n\n### 2.2 Application Structure\n\n**Entry Point**: `App.tsx` (395 lines)\n- Renders navbar with zoom controls\n- Sidebar with 50 trains (clickable navigation)\n- TimelineHeader (date navigation)\n- InfiniteCanvas (R3F rendering)\n- Minimap (bird's eye view)\n- TaskTooltip (hover info)\n- TaskDetailsPanel (selection)\n- PerformancePanel (FPS stats)\n\n**Canvas Rendering**: `InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (2106 lines - VERY LARGE)\n- OrthographicCamera setup\n- Pan/zoom interaction handling\n- LOD (Level of Detail) system with smooth transitions\n- Three layers: MacroLayer, MesoLayer, MicroLayer\n- Viewport culling with RBush spatial index\n- Momentum/inertial panning\n- Animation state machine\n\n**Layers**:\n- **MacroLayer** (Heatmap): Visible at zoom < 0.5. Uses InstancedMesh + d3-scale-chromatic for yellowâ†’orangeâ†’red gradient\n- **MesoLayer** (Task Blocks): Visible at zoom 0.5-2.0. InstancedMesh for all task blocks with conflict indicators\n- **MicroLayer** (Detailed Cards): Visible at zoom > 2.0. HTML overlays with task details, grid lines, labels\n\n**State Management**: `state/`\n- `canvas-machine.ts`: Creates state machine with two slices (viewport, interaction)\n- `viewport.ts`: Camera position, momentum, animation, canvas size\n- `interaction.ts`: Hover/selection/focus state for tasks\n- Uses `@zazcart/tools` with `metadata: false` for 60fps optimization\n\n### 2.3 Data & Types\n\n**Mock Data Generator** (`data/generator.ts`):\n- 50 trains (30 ALSTOM, 20 KINKI Sharyo)\n- 10 years of tasks per train\n- ~8-12 tasks per train per year\n- ~5% have conflict flags\n- Generated on-demand (no persistence)\n\n**Domain Types** (`data/types.ts`):\n- `Train`: id, name, assetType\n- `Task`: id, trainId, startDate, endDate, type, hasConflict, predecessorId\n- `TaskRender`: Computed positions (trainIndex, startDay, duration, color)\n- `WorldBounds`: Navigation limits\n- `ViewConfig`: Pixel scales (pxPerDay=10, pxPerTrain=24)\n\n---\n\n## 3. STATE MANAGEMENT IMPLEMENTATION (@zazcart/tools)\n\n### 3.1 Usage Pattern\n\n```typescript\n// canvas-machine.ts\nexport const canvasMachine = createMachine({\n  id: 'canvas',\n  slices: {\n    viewport: viewportSlice,      // Camera, momentum, animation\n    interaction: interactionSlice, // Hover, selection, focus\n  },\n  metadata: false, // âڑ ï¸ڈ CRITICAL: Disables metadata tracking for 60fps\n});\n\n// Component access\nconst useCanvas = canvasMachine.useMachine;\nconst useCanvasSelector = canvasMachine.useSelector;\nconst canvasStore = { getState, dispatch, subscribe };\n```\n\n### 3.2 Viewport Slice (viewport.ts)\n\n**State**:\n- `camera`: { x, y } - world coordinates\n- `canvasSize`: { width, height }\n- `isInitialized`: boolean\n- `momentum`: { velocityX, velocityY, isActive }\n- `animation`: { isAnimating, startTime, duration, startX/Y/Zoom, targetX/Y/Zoom }\n- `isPanning`: boolean\n- Pan start/camera positions\n\n**Reducers**:\n- `setCamera(pos)`, `setCameraX(x)`, `setCameraY(y)`\n- `setCanvasSize(size)`\n- `initialize({ camera, canvasSize })`\n- `startMomentum`, `updateMomentum`, `stopMomentum`\n- `startAnimation`, `updateAnimation`, `stopAnimation`\n- `startPan`, `updatePan`, `endPan`\n- `reset()`\n\n### 3.3 Interaction Slice (interaction.ts)\n\n**State**:\n- `hoveredTaskId`: string | null (F018)\n- `mouseWorldPos`: { x, y } | null\n- `selectedTaskId`: string | null (F019)\n- `focusedTaskId`: string | null (F021 - keyboard navigation)\n- `isHoverable`: boolean\n\n**Reducers**:\n- `setHoveredTask(id)`, `clearHover()`\n- `setSelectedTask(id)`, `clearSelection()`\n- `setFocusedTask(id)`, `clearFocus()`\n- `setMouseWorldPos(pos)`\n- `reset()`\n\n---\n\n## 4. CANVAS/R3F IMPLEMENTATION\n\n### 4.1 LOD System\n\n**Three-Layer Architecture** with smooth blending:\n\n| LOD | Zoom | Render Method | Visibility |\n|-----|------|---------------|-----------|\n| **MACRO** | < 0.5x | Heatmap (InstancedMesh) | Strategic overview - monthly cells |\n| **MESO** | 0.5-2.0x | Task blocks (InstancedMesh) | Default view - DNA pattern |\n| **MICRO** | > 2.0x | Detail cards (Html) | Detailed inspection - labels, grid |\n\n**Blend Zones** (آ±0.15 around thresholds):\n- MACRO â†’ MESO at 0.35-0.65 zoom\n- MESO â†’ MICRO at 1.85-2.15 zoom\n- Smooth opacity transitions between layers\n\n**Implementation**:\n- `calculateLayerOpacities(zoom)` â†’ { macro, meso, micro } opacities\n- `LODLayers` component renders all visible layers with interpolated opacity\n- Each layer controls its own visibility/culling\n\n### 4.2 Performance Optimizations\n\n1. **InstancedMesh Pre-allocation** (PERF-001):\n   - Single draw call per layer (instead of 1000s)\n   - Pre-allocate 100k instances max\n   - Use `mesh.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate` for dynamic updates\n\n2. **Viewport Culling** (PERF-002):\n   - RBushSpatialIndex for O(log n) queries\n   - `calculateViewportBounds()` expands render viewport slightly\n   - MicroLayer: `filterVisibleTasks()` reduces HTML elements\n   - Limit to 50 HTML labels in MicroLayer\n\n3. **Object Pooling**:\n   - Reuse Object3D temp objects in loops\n   - Avoid allocations in animation loops\n\n4. **GC Avoidance**:\n   - `@zazcart/tools` configured with `metadata: false`\n   - Prevents object allocations on every dispatch\n   - Direct state mutations (Immer-based)\n\n### 4.3 Interaction Handling\n\n**Pan**:\n- Mouse drag updates camera position\n- Bounds-checked to world limits\n- Momentum physics option (currently disabled - BUG-008)\n\n**Zoom**:\n- Scroll wheel (Google Maps-style)\n- Trackpad pinch support (with workarounds for BUG-009)\n- Zoom centered on cursor position\n- Smooth animation (250ms) vs direct application (trackpad)\n\n**Gestures**:\n- Keyboard: Arrow keys for pan, +/- for zoom (F027, F028)\n- Click: Select tasks (F019)\n- Hover: Highlight tasks + tooltip (F018, F020)\n- Keyboard focus: Navigate tasks (F021)\n\n---\n\n## 5. Uses InstancedMesh + d3-scale-chromatic for yellowâ†’orangeâ†’red gradient\n- **MesoLayer** (Task Blocks): Visible at zoom 0.5-2.0. InstancedMesh for all task blocks with conflict indicators\n- **MicroLayer** (Detailed Cards): Visible at zoom > 2.0. HTML overlays with task details, grid lines, labels\n\n**State Management**: `state/`\n- `canvas-machine.ts`: Creates state machine with two slices (viewport, interaction)\n- `viewport.ts`: Camera position, momentum, animation, canvas size\n- `interaction.ts`: Hover/selection/focus state for tasks\n- Uses `@zazcart/tools` with `metadata: false` for 60fps optimization\n\n### 2.3 Data & Types\n\n**Mock Data Generator** (`data/generator.ts`):\n- 50 trains (30 ALSTOM, 20 KINKI Sharyo)\n- 10 years of tasks per train\n- ~8-12 tasks per train per year\n- ~5% have conflict flags\n- Generated on-demand (no persistence)\n\n**Domain Types** (`data/types.ts`):\n- `Train`: id, name, assetType\n- `Task`: id, trainId, startDate, endDate, type, hasConflict, predecessorId\n- `TaskRender`: Computed positions (trainIndex, startDay, duration, color)\n- `WorldBounds`: Navigation limits\n- `ViewConfig`: Pixel scales (pxPerDay=10, pxPerTrain=24)\n\n---\n\n## 3. STATE MANAGEMENT IMPLEMENTATION (@zazcart/tools)\n\n### 3.1 Usage Pattern\n\n```typescript\n// canvas-machine.ts\nexport const canvasMachine = createMachine({\n  id: 'canvas',\n  slices: {\n    viewport: viewportSlice,      // Camera, momentum, animation\n    interaction: interactionSlice, // Hover, selection, focus\n  },\n  metadata: false, // âڑ ï¸ڈ CRITICAL: Disables metadata tracking for 60fps\n});\n\n// Component access\nconst useCanvas = canvasMachine.useMachine;\nconst useCanvasSelector = canvasMachine.useSelector;\nconst canvasStore = { getState, dispatch, subscribe };\n```\n\n### 3.2 Viewport Slice (viewport.ts)\n\n**State**:\n- `camera`: { x, y } - world coordinates\n- `canvasSize`: { width, height }\n- `isInitialized`: boolean\n- `momentum`: { velocityX, velocityY, isActive }\n- `animation`: { isAnimating, startTime, duration, startX/Y/Zoom, targetX/Y/Zoom }\n- `isPanning`: boolean\n- Pan start/camera positions\n\n**Reducers**:\n- `setCamera(pos)`, `setCameraX(x)`, `setCameraY(y)`\n- `setCanvasSize(size)`\n- `initialize({ camera, canvasSize })`\n- `startMomentum`, `updateMomentum`, `stopMomentum`\n- `startAnimation`, `updateAnimation`, `stopAnimation`\n- `startPan`, `updatePan`, `endPan`\n- `reset()`\n\n### 3.3 Interaction Slice (interaction.ts)\n\n**State**:\n- `hoveredTaskId`: string | null (F018)\n- `mouseWorldPos`: { x, y } | null\n- `selectedTaskId`: string | null (F019)\n- `focusedTaskId`: string | null (F021 - keyboard navigation)\n- `isHoverable`: boolean\n\n**Reducers**:\n- `setHoveredTask(id)`, `clearHover()`\n- `setSelectedTask(id)`, `clearSelection()`\n- `setFocusedTask(id)`, `clearFocus()`\n- `setMouseWorldPos(pos)`\n- `reset()`\n\n---\n\n## 4. CANVAS/R3F IMPLEMENTATION\n\n### 4.1 LOD System\n\n**Three-Layer Architecture** with smooth blending:\n\n| LOD | Zoom | Render Method | Visibility |\n|-----|------|---------------|-----------|\n| **MACRO** | < 0.5x | Heatmap (InstancedMesh) | Strategic overview - monthly cells |\n| **MESO** | 0.5-2.0x | Task blocks (InstancedMesh) | Default view - DNA pattern |\n| **MICRO** | > 2.0x | Detail cards (Html) | Detailed inspection - labels, grid |\n\n**Blend Zones** (آ±0.15 around thresholds):\n- MACRO â†’ MESO at 0.35-0.65 zoom\n- MESO â†’ MICRO at 1.85-2.15 zoom\n- Smooth opacity transitions between layers\n\n**Implementation**:\n- `calculateLayerOpacities(zoom)` â†’ { macro, meso, micro } opacities\n- `LODLayers` component renders all visible layers with interpolated opacity\n- Each layer controls its own visibility/culling\n\n### 4.2 Performance Optimizations\n\n1. **InstancedMesh Pre-allocation** (PERF-001):\n   - Single draw call per layer (instead of 1000s)\n   - Pre-allocate 100k instances max\n   - Use `mesh.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate` for dynamic updates\n\n2. **Viewport Culling** (PERF-002):\n   - RBushSpatialIndex for O(log n) queries\n   - `calculateViewportBounds()` expands render viewport slightly\n   - MicroLayer: `filterVisibleTasks()` reduces HTML elements\n   - Limit to 50 HTML labels in MicroLayer\n\n3. **Object Pooling**:\n   - Reuse Object3D temp objects in loops\n   - Avoid allocations in animation loops\n\n4. **GC Avoidance**:\n   - `@zazcart/tools` configured with `metadata: false`\n   - Prevents object allocations on every dispatch\n   - Direct state mutations (Immer-based)\n\n### 4.3 Interaction Handling\n\n**Pan**:\n- Mouse drag updates camera position\n- Bounds-checked to world limits\n- Momentum physics option (currently disabled - BUG-008)\n\n**Zoom**:\n- Scroll wheel (Google Maps-style)\n- Trackpad pinch support (with workarounds for BUG-009)\n- Zoom centered on cursor position\n- Smooth animation (250ms) vs direct application (trackpad)\n\n**Gestures**:\n- Keyboard: Arrow keys for pan, +/- for zoom (F027, F028)\n- Click: Select tasks (F019)\n- Hover: Highlight tasks + tooltip (F018, F020)\n- Keyboard focus: Navigate tasks (F021)\n\n---\n\n## 5. IDENTIFIED","-","-**Objective:**","-Deep research and planning for viewport culling optimization + Investigation of trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook.","-","-**Research Conducted:**","-- Web searches: Three.js frustum culling, R3F viewport culling, RBush spatial indexing, deck.gl massive rendering, Figma WebGL architecture","-- Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)","-- Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant)","-- Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation)","-","-**Key Findings:**","-","-1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport","-   - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame","-   - No built-in per-instance frustum culling","-","-2. **RBush Spatial Index:**","-   - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time)","-   - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms","-   - Perfect for viewport culling queries","-","-3. **Culling Strategy:**","-   - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer","-   - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered)","-   - No new allocations during pan/zoom","-","-4. **Alternative Libraries:**","-   - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling","-   - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting","-   - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data","-","-**Documents Created:**","-- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Comprehensive research consolidating all findings","-","-**Task Created: PERF-002**","-","-Added to features.json with 4 implementation phases:","-","-1. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree for task/entity lookup","-2. **Phase 2 - Culled InstancedMesh:** Instance reordering + count manipulation","-3. **Phase 3 - Layer Integration:** MesoLayer, MacroLayer viewport-aware","-4. **Phase 4 - Validation:** Performance testing and profiling","-","-**Dependency Update:**","-- STATE-ARCH-001 now `blockedBy: \"PERF-002\"`","-- PERF-002 is incremental improvement that can be done BEFORE full architecture migration","-- Establishes viewport culling foundation that STATE-ARCH-001 will build upon","-","-**Performance Targets:**","-- Viewport query: <5ms for 1000 results","-- Instance update: <1ms for 1000 visible","-- FPS: 60fps constant during pan/zoom","-- GPU instances: Only visible count (not all 60k)","-","-**BUG-009 Investigation (Added):**","-- User reported: Trackpad zoom has lag/stutter, but minimap navigation is smooth","-- Diagnosis: This is INPUT HANDLING issue, not rendering (minimap proves rendering is fine)","-- Root cause candidates:","-  - ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms creates queued animations","-  - ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms delays execution","-  - animateZoomTo() starts new RAF instead of updating target","-- Solution options:","-  - Option A: Remove animation for trackpad (direct zoom like minimap)","-  - Option B: Update target during animation","-  - Option C: Use d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react (market solutions)","-","-**Files Changed:**","-- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` (NEW - comprehensive research)","-- `features.json`:","-  - Added PERF-002 with 5 phases (phase0 = BUG-009 fix first)","-  - Added BUG-009 with detailed investigation","-  - Updated STATE-ARCH-001 blockedBy: PERF-002","-- `claude-progress.txt` (this file)","-","-**Alignment Fix (Added):**","-- PERF-002.Phase1 was duplicating STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 (both had RBush)","-- Fix: PERF-002.Phase1 now defines ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only + placeholder","-- STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays","-- No more duplicate work - tasks are now complementary","-","-**Status:** Planning complete. Tasks aligned. COMPLETE","-","-**Problem:**","-Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.","-","-**Root Cause Analysis:**","-React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:","-1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state","-2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position","-3. New drag starts from stale position â†’ canvas \"jumps\" back","-","-**Solution Implemented:**","-1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef","-2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state","-3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:","-   - `handleCameraInitialized`","-   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)","-   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)","-   - `handlePinchZoom`","-   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)","-   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)","-4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state","-","-**Files Changed:**","-- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)","-- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)","-","-**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**","-- âœ… Single drag pans correctly","-- âœ… Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect","-- âœ… Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning","-- âœ… No console errors","-","-### Harness Updates","-","-**F117 Archived:**","-- \"Full visual parity with POC reference\" no longer scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE","-","-**Objective:**","-Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.","-","-**Implementation:**","-","-1. **deltaMode Normalization:**","-   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)","-   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)","-   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly","-","-2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**","-   - Track event frequency with timestamps","-   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior","-   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad","-   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode","-","-3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**","-   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003","-   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)","-","-4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**","-   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5","-   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events","-","-**Constants Added:**","-```typescript","-LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line","-PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page","-MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity","-TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad","-MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event","-TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events","-TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm","-```","-","-**Files Changed:**","-- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (normalizeWheelDelta function, updated handleWheel)","-- `features.json` (F120, F121 passes: âœ… Smooth zoom animation","-","-**Next:** F116 (Performance), F117 (Visual parity)","-","----","-","-## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE âœ…","-","-### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE","-","-**Objective:**","-Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.","-","-**Implementation:**","-","-1. **Velocity Tracking:**","-   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps","-   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation","-   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity","-","-2. **Momentum Animation:**","-   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples","-   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity","-   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down","-   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units","-","-3. **Integration:**","-   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)","-   - Cleanup animation on component unmount","-   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation","-","-**Constants Used:**","-```typescript","-MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier","-MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold","-MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average","-```","-","-**Files Changed:**","-- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)","-- `features.json` @zazcart/tools Critical Performance Fixes - ALL COMPLETE\n\n**Objective:**\nComplete audit and fix all performance issues in @zazcart/tools state machine library identified in FIXME files.\n\n**Fixes Applied (7 total):**\n\n1. **Unsafe Listener Unsubscription** (store.ts)\n   - Copy listeners array before iteration to prevent issues during unsubscribe\n   - Prevents ConcurrentModificationException-like bugs\n\n2. **Validation on Hot Path** (context.ts)\n   - Skip schema validation in production for performance\n   - Only validate in development (NODE_ENV !== 'production')\n\n3. **devLog Crash Risk** (logs.ts)\n   - Safe error handling instead of throwing in devLog\n\n4. **O(Nآ²) reduceObject** (object-utils.ts)\n   - Changed from spread operator in loop to Object.assign\n   - O(N) implementation now\n\n5. **GC Thrashing - Metadata Option** (machine.ts, models/machine.ts)\n   - Added `metadata?: boolean` option to MachineConfig\n   - When `false`, skips metadata updates on dispatch\n   - Prevents GC thrashing from object allocations at 60fps\n\n6. **V8 De-optimization** (hooks.ts)\n   - Clean object return instead of hybrid array+object\n   - Breaking change: `useMachine()` returns `{ state, actions, dispatch }`\n\n7. **Double Proxying** (machine.ts)\n   - Direct assignment instead of Immer produce() for selectors\n   - Reduces proxy overhead in hot path\n\n**FIXME Files Deleted:**\n- packages/toolkit/FIXME_STATE_MACHINE.md\n- packages/toolkit/FIXME_HELPERS.md\n- packages/toolkit/FIXME_SCHEMA.md\n- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/FIXME.md\n\n**Canvas Integration:**\n- Applied `metadata: false` to canvas-machine.ts for 60fps optimization\n\n**TypeScript Fixes:**\n- Fixed reduceObject return type in object-utils.ts\n- Fixed check() return type in context.ts\n\n**Tests:**\n- 216 pass, 4 fail (pre-existing stack trace test failures in error-utils)\n\n**QA Visual Verification (Playwright Impact:**\n- ~300 lines of code can be replaced\n- ~26KB bundle increase (tree-shakeable)\n- BUG-009 potentially resolved via d3-zoom\n- 60-70% reduction in event handling complexity\n\n**Files with D3 Opportunities:**\n1. **InfiniteCanvas.tsx (HIGH)** - d3-zoom for normalizeWheelDelta, trackpad detection, pinch\n2. **TimelineHeader.tsx (MEDIUM)** - d3-time, d3-time-format, d3-scale\n3. **Minimap.tsx (MEDIUM)** - d3-scale, d3-interpolate\n4. **MacroLayer.tsx (LOW)** - d3-scale-chromatic, d3-array\n5. **types.ts (LOW)** - d3-array (extent)\n6. **generator.ts (OPTIONAL)** - d3-time (not critical)\n\n**Files WITHOUT D3 Opportunities:**\n- viewport.ts (architecture sound with @zazcart/tools)\n- App.tsx (orchestration layer)\n- MesoLayer.tsx (R3F-specific)\n- MicroLayer.tsx (React/CSS domain)\n- PerfMonitor.tsx, TaskTooltip.tsx, TaskDetailsPanel.tsx (UI components)\n\n**Phased Migration Plan:**\n- **Phase 1 (Quick Wins):** types.ts, TimelineHeader.tsx, Minimap.tsx\n- PerformancePanel positioned at `top: 50` overlapped TimelineHeader\n\nFix:\n- Added `zIndex: 2` to labels and background color for readability\n- Reduced gradient overlay width from 30px to 20px with `zIndex: 1`\n- Moved PerformancePanel from \"top-left\" to \"bottom-left\" position\n\n**BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance Issue - FIXED**\n\nProblem: Trackpad pinch-to-zoom on MacBook had ~300ms latency causing perceived lag.\n\nRoot Cause:\n- 50ms accumulation timeout to batch wheel events\n- 250ms animation duration per zoom operation\n- Multiple rapid pinches queued animations\n\nFix:\n- Modified `handleWheel` ctrlKey branch to apply zoom DIRECTLY\n- Removed accumulation timeout for trackpad pinch events\n- Removed animation delay - zoom applied immediately like `handlePinchZoom`\n- Cursor-centered zoom preserved (world point stays fixed)\n\nAlso refactored handleWheel to Google Maps-like behavior:\n- 2-finger scroll = pan (deltaX + deltaY)\n- Ctrl+scroll (pinch) = zoom toward cursor\n\n**Files Changed:**\n- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` - Sync Loop Scheduler:** Fixed execution order, physics/render decoupled\n5. **Phase 5 - Object Pooling & GC Elimination:** Closures, for loops, pre-compute\n6. **Phase 6 - Mobile Optimizations:** Passive listeners, iOS limits (256MB/380MB)\n7. **Phase 7 - Undo/Redo:** Command Pattern + Immer patches\n8. **Phase 8 - Worker Layer:** Optional, OffscreenCanvas + SharedArrayBuffer\n\n**Critical Decision: @zazcart/tools over Zustand**\n\nPDF recommends Zustand, but this project uses **@zazcart/tools** exclusively:\n- Documented in 3 places with \"NUNCA Zustand\" warning\n- Clarifications added to phases explaining Simulation vs UI state separation\n- @zazcart/tools is ONLY for UI state (<30fps, event-driven)\n- Simulation State uses TypedArrays with direct access (60fps)\n\n**Verification Completed:**\n- All 11 PDF sections mapped to implementation phases\n- All recommendations from Sections 2-9 covered\n- Risk matrix mitigations included\n- Performance targets defined (100k entities, 60fps, <100MB, <5ms queries)\n\n**Files Changed:**\n- `features.json` - IDENTIFIED\n\n**Objective:**\nDeep research and planning for viewport culling optimization + Investigation of trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook.\n\n**Research Conducted:**\n- Web searches: Three.js frustum culling, R3F viewport culling, RBush spatial indexing, deck.gl massive rendering, Figma WebGL architecture\n- Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)\n- Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant)\n- Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation)\n\n**Key Findings:**\n\n1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport\n   - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame\n   - No built-in per-instance frustum culling\n\n2. **RBush Spatial Index:**\n   - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time)\n   - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms\n   - Perfect for viewport culling queries\n\n3. **Culling Strategy:**\n   - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer\n   - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered)\n   - No new allocations during pan/zoom\n\n4. **Alternative Libraries:**\n   - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling\n   - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting\n   - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data\n\n**Documents Created:**\n- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Comprehensive research consolidating all findings\n\n**Task Created: PERF-002**\n\nAdded to features.json with 4 implementation phases:\n\n1. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree for task/entity lookup\n2. **Phase 2 - Culled InstancedMesh:** Instance reordering + count manipulation\n3. **Phase 3 - Layer Integration:** MesoLayer, MacroLayer viewport-aware\n4. **Phase 4 - Validation:** Performance testing and profiling\n\n**Dependency Update:**\n- STATE-ARCH-001 now `blockedBy: \"PERF-002\"`\n- PERF-002 is incremental improvement that can be done BEFORE full architecture migration\n- Establishes viewport culling foundation that STATE-ARCH-001 will build upon\n\n**Performance Targets:**\n- Viewport query: <5ms for 1000 results\n- Instance update: <1ms for 1000 visible\n- FPS: 60fps constant during pan/zoom\n- GPU instances: Only visible count (not all 60k)\n\n**BUG-009 Investigation (Added):**\n- User reported: Trackpad zoom has lag/stutter, but minimap navigation is smooth\n- Diagnosis: This is INPUT HANDLING issue, not rendering (minimap proves rendering is fine)\n- Root cause candidates:\n  - ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms creates queued animations\n  - ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms delays execution\n  - animateZoomTo() starts new RAF instead of updating target\n- Solution options:\n  - Option A: Remove animation for trackpad (direct zoom like minimap)\n  - Option B: Update target during animation\n  - Option C: Use d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react (market solutions)\n\n**Files Changed:**\n- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` (NEW - comprehensive research)\n- `features.json`:\n  - Added PERF-002 with 5 phases (phase0 = BUG-009 fix first)\n  - Added BUG-009 with detailed investigation\n  - Updated STATE-ARCH-001 blockedBy: PERF-002\n- `claude-progress.txt` (this file)\n\n**Alignment Fix (Added):**\n- PERF-002.Phase1 was duplicating STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 (both had RBush)\n- Fix: PERF-002.Phase1 now defines ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only + placeholder\n- STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays\n- No more duplicate work - tasks are now complementary\n\n**Status:** Planning complete. Tasks aligned. Ready for implementation.\n\n---\n\n## Session: 2025-12-17 (STATE-ARCH-001 Planning) - COMPLETE âœ…\n\n### STATE-ARCH-001: High-Performance State Management Architecture - PLANNED\n\n**Objective:**\nPlan refactoring of state management architecture based on research document analyzing Figma, Google Maps, CesiumJS, and WebGL game engines.\n\n**Research Document:**\n- `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf`\n- Key finding: GC (Garbage Collection) is the primary bottleneck, not rendering\n- React Hooks and reactive stores inadequate for 60fps on mobile\n- Recommends Dual-Store: Simulation State (TypedArrays) + Application State (external store)\n\n**Task Created: STATE-ARCH-001**\n\nAdded comprehensive task to features.json with 5 implementation phases:\n\n1. **Phase 1 - Foundation:** Create EntityStore with TypedArrays (Structure of Arrays pattern)\n2. **Phase 2 - Spatial Index:** Implement RBush R-tree for viewport queries\n3. **Phase 3 - UI Bridge:** Connect Engine State to @zazcart/tools (NOT Zustand as PDF suggests)\n4. **Phase 4 - Object Pooling:** Eliminate GC pauses in animation loops\n5. **Phase 5 - Undo/Redo:** Command Pattern + Immer patches\n\n**CRITICAL DECISION: @zazcart/tools over Zustand**\n\nThe PDF recommends Zustand for UI state, but this project uses **@zazcart/tools** (packages/toolkit):\n- Already implemented and integrated\n- Uses Immer for immutability\n- Uses useSyncExternalStore for synchronous updates\n- Has slice-based architecture matching project needs\n\n**Files Changed:**\n- `features.json` - Added STATE-ARCH-001 task with detailed implementation plan\n- `CLAUDE.md` - Added \"High-Performance Architecture\" section with guidelines\n\n**Performance Targets:**\n- 100,000+ entities renderable\n- 60fps constant during pan/zoom\n- <100MB memory for 100k entities\n- <5ms viewport queries\n- Zero perceptible GC pauses\n\n**Next Steps:**\n- Implementation will be phased over multiple sessions\n- Start with Phase 1 (EntityStore) when prioritized\n\n---\n\n## Session: 2025-12-17 (UX Bug Fixes) - Wave/group configuration with conflict analysis\n- `scripts/parallel-runner.ts` - Interactive parallel execution runner\n- `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` - Complete documentation\n\n**Files Modified:**\n- `.gitignore` - Added `.trees/` for worktree directories\n- `features.json` - Added `parallelExecution` section with waves configuration\n\n**Key Features of Parallel Runner:**\n1. **Git worktree management** - Creates isolated working directories\n2. **Terminal session spawning** - Opens separate Claude sessions per feature\n3. **Isolation prompts** - Each session restricted to its feature scope\n4. **Wave dependencies** - Prevents starting Wave N before Wave N-1 completes\n5. **Merge helper** - Automates feature branch merging\n6. **Status display** - Shows progress across all waves/groups\n7. **macOS notifications** - Alerts for session events\n8. **Cleanup command** - Removes all worktrees\n\n**Speedup Calculation:**\n- Sequential: 10 features = 10 sessions\n- Parallel: 4 waves (2+2+1+1) = ~4 \"effective sessions\"\n- Estimated speedup: **2.5x**\n\n**Usage:**\n```bash\n# Show status\nbun scripts/parallel-runner.ts status\n\n# Execute Wave 1\nbun scripts/parallel-runner.ts wave 1\n\n# Merge completed feature\nbun scripts/parallel-runner.ts merge F023\n```\n\n---\n\n## Session: 2025-12-17 (F020 Tooltip) - COMPLETE\n\n**Objective:**\nAdd real-time WebGL performance metrics tool with detailed logging for both visual and programmatic analysis.\n\n**Implementation:**\n\n1. **r3f-perf Integration:**\n   - Installed r3f-perf 7.2.3 via `bun add r3f-perf`\n   - Visual panel shows: GPU, CPU, FPS, draw calls, triangles, geometries, textures, shaders\n\n2. **PerfMonitor Component (`apps/canvas/src/components/PerfMonitor.tsx`):**\n   - ~400 lines of code with types, logging system, and visual panel\n   - PerfLogger sub-component tracks metrics every frame\n   - Circular buffer stores last 60s of performance data\n   - Rolling averages calculated over 60-frame windows\n\n3. **Global Console API:**\n   - `window.__PERF_LOG__` - Access log buffer (last 60s)\n   - `window.__PERF_EXPORT__()` - Export full log as array\n   - `window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name)` - Start recording session\n   - `window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()` - Stop and get aggregated stats\n   - `window.__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__()` - Check active session\n   - `window.__PERF_CLEAR__()` - Clear log buffer\n\n4. **Session Statistics:**\n   - FPS: avg, min, max, p1 (worst 1%), p99 (best 99%)\n   - MS: avg, max frame time\n   - Frame counts: below 30fps, below 60fps\n   - Duration and total frames\n\n5. **UI Integration:**\n   - Toggle visibility via 'P' key\n   - \"Perf\" button in navbar\n   - Panel positioned top-right\n   - Runs in development mode by default\n\n**Known Issue:**\n- r3f-perf causes console spam with `expandShaderIncludes TypeError`\n- Root cause: troika-three-utils 0.49.0 incompatible with Three.js 0.170+\n- Fix exists in troika-three-utils 0.50.3+ but r3f-perf hasn't updated\n- Tracked in: https://github.com/utsuboco/r3f-perf/issues/58\n- Does NOT affect functionality - panel and logging work correctly\n\n**Files Created/Modified:**\n- `apps/canvas/src/components/PerfMonitor.tsx` (NEW)\n- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (perf state, toggle, event tracking)\n- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (PerfMonitor props)\n- `docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md` (NEW - comprehensive documentation)\n- `features.json` (PERF-001 passes: true)\n\n**Testing:**\n- Visual panel renders correctly (verified via Playwright MCP)\n- Session API works: START_SESSION, SESSION_STATUS, STOP_SESSION\n- Performance logging active even when panel hidden\n\n**Commit:**\n- `feat(canvas): PERF-001 - add r3f-perf performance monitor with logging system`\n\n---\n\n## Session: 2025-12-16 (Harness Tasks Creation) - COMPLETE âœ…\n\n### Novas Tarefas Adicionadas ao Harness\n\n**Contexto:**\nUsuأ،rio solicitou criaأ§أ£o de vأ،rias tarefas importantes para melhorar o harness e a qualidade do cأ³digo.\n\n**Tarefas Criadas:**\n\n1. **URGENT-001** (Priority 0 - COMPLETE\n\n**Problem:**\nCanvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.\n\n**Root Cause Analysis:**\nReact state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:\n1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state\n2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position\n3. New drag starts from stale position â†’ canvas \"jumps\" back\n\n**Solution Implemented:**\n1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef\n2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state\n3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:\n   - `handleCameraInitialized`\n   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)\n   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)\n   - `handlePinchZoom`\n   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)\n   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)\n4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state\n\n**Files Changed:**\n- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)\n- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)\n\n**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**\n- âœ… Single drag pans correctly\n- âœ… Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect\n- âœ… Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning\n- âœ… No console errors\n\n### Harness Updates\n\n**F117 Archived:**\n- \"Full visual parity with POC reference\" no longer relevant\n- âœ… Smooth animation with cubicOut easing\n- âœ… No console errors\n\n### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE\n\n**Objective:**\nNormalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.\n\n**Implementation:**\n\n1. **deltaMode Normalization:**\n   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)\n   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)\n   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly\n\n2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**\n   - Track event frequency with timestamps\n   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior\n   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad\n   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode\n\n3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**\n   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003\n   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)\n\n4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**\n   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5\n   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events\n\n**Constants Added:**\n```typescript\nLINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line\nPAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page\nMOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity\nTRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad\nMAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event\nTRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events\nTRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm\n```\n\n**Files Changed:**\n- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (normalizeWheelDelta function, updated handleWheel)\n- Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE\n\n**Objective:**\nImplement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.\n\n**Implementation:**\n\n1. **Velocity Tracking:**\n   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps\n   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation\n   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity\n\n2. **Momentum Animation:**\n   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples\n   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity\n   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down\n   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units\n\n3. **Integration:**\n   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)\n   - Cleanup animation on component unmount\n   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation\n\n**Constants Used:**\n```typescript\nMOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier\nMOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold\nMOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average\n```\n\n**Files Changed:**\n- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)\n- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)\n\n**Visual Tests (Playwright SignatureRail (UK, Modaxo Group)\n   100â†’\n   101â†’### Overview\n   102â†’Train timetable and resource planning since 1994. Global presence with TrainPlan flagship product.\n   103â†’\n   104â†’### TrainPlan Features\n   105â†’- Timetable generation considering all resources and constraints\n   106â†’- Optimize capacity\n   107â†’- Reduce vehicle and operator costs\n   108â†’- Improve services\n   109â†’\n   110â†’### User Testimonial (Metro-North Railroad)\n   111â†’> \"TrainPlan allows us to manage our data in an efficient manner allowing for safe and high performing service changes to be implemented as quickly as possible.\"\n   112â†’\n   113â†’### Marey Chart Visualization (Industry Gold Standard)\n   114â†’\n   115â†’#### What is a Marey Chart?\n   116â†’- Invented by أ‰tienne-Jules Marey (1878) based on Mr. Ibry's method\n   117â†’- **Axes**: Distance (Y) vs Time (X)\n   118â†’- **Trains**: Diagonal lines - slope = speed\n   119â†’- **Direction**: Top-left to bottom-right (one direction), bottom-left to top-right (reverse)\n   120â†’- **Stops**: Horizontal sections indicate stop duration\n   121â†’- **Crossings**: Lines crossing = trains passing each other\n   122â†’\n   123â†’#### Why Marey Charts are Superior\n   124â†’- \"Far superior representation for finding late trains and understanding delay propagation\"\n   125â†’- Both time and space dimensions in compact form\n   126â†’- Used as cover of Tufte's \"The Visual Display of Quantitative Information\"\n   127â†’- Speed immediately visible from line slope\n   128â†’\n   129â†’#### Modern Implementations\n   130â†’- [D3.js Observable Example](https://observablehq.com/@d3/mareys-trains)\n   131â†’- [JointJS Demo](https://www.jointjs.com/demos/marey-chart)\n   132â†’- [Tableau Public](https://public.tableau.com/en-us/s/blog/2015/08/marey-inspired-path-train-schedule)\n   133â†’\n   134â†’### UI Patterns to Steal\n   135â†’- Marey chart for train scheduling visualization\n   136â†’- What-if scenario management\n   137â†’- Constraint-aware scheduling\n   138â†’\n   139â†’---\n   140â†’\n   141â†’## 4. Other Notable Competitors\n   142â†’\n   143â†’### PTV Visum\n   144â†’- **Scenario management**: Create and manage infrastructure modifications\n   145â†’- **Fleet planning**: Depot capacities, turning times, empty runs\n   146â†’- **Batch evaluation**: Compare scenarios programmatically\n   147â†’\n   148â†’### TBEST (Free Desktop Software)\n   149â†’- **GIS-based**: Integrates socio-economic, land use, transit network\n   150â†’- **Scenario planning**: True network-based future year simulation\n   151â†’\n   152â†’### AnyLogic\n   153â†’- **Simulation-based optimization**: Two-layer approach\n   154â†’- **What-if scenarios**: Assess economic effects of changes\n   155â†’- **Replacement service planning**: For scheduled shutdowns\n   156â†’\n   157â†’### Tractian CMMS\n   158â†’- **AI-driven workload balancing**: Eliminates scheduling conflicts\n   159â†’- **Real-time technician availability**: Auto task prioritization\n   160â†’- **Visual Scheduling**: Plan by technician, area, or shift\n   161â†’- **Overload detection**: See idle time instantly\n   162â†’\n   163â†’### FaultFixers\n   164â†’- **Drag-and-drop calendar**: Intuitive ticket assignment\n   165â†’- **Gantt Chart view**: See everyone's schedule at once\n   166â†’\n   167â†’---\n   168â†’\n   169â†’## 5. Gestalt Principles for Dashboard Design\n   201â†’\n   202â†’### Core Principles\n   203â†’\n   204â†’1. **Proximity**\n   205â†’   - Group related filters together\n   206â†’   - Place filter near its affected view\n   207â†’   - Example: Fleet status filters grouped with fleet list\n   208â†’\n   209â†’2. **Similarity**\n   210â†’   - Same colors for same data types\n   211â†’   - Consistent shapes for similar metrics\n   212â†’   - Example: All \"fit\" trains in green, all \"unfit\" in red\n   213â†’\n   214â†’3. **Enclosure**\n   215â†’   - Cards/boxes for related metrics\n   216â†’   - Clear distinction between data sets\n   217â†’   - Example: KPI cards with distinct borders\n   218â†’\n   219â†’4. **Connectedness**\n   220â†’   - Lines linking related elements\n   221â†’   - Network and line graph patterns\n   222â†’   - Example: Dependency lines between tasks\n   223â†’\n   224â†’5. **Closure**\n   225â†’   - Minimal axis lines (just X and Y enough)\n   226â†’   - User perceives complete structure\n   227â†’   - Example: Bar charts without full enclosure\n   228â†’\n   229â†’6. **Continuity**\n   230â†’   - Sort bars large to small\n   231â†’   - Time charts past to future\n   232â†’   - Perceived as continuous whole\n   233â†’\n   234â†’### Application to MMS\n   235â†’- Group maintenance metrics together (proximity)\n   236â†’- Color-code by fleet type and task status (similarity)\n   237â†’- Use cards for KPIs with clear borders (enclosure)\n   238â†’- Show task dependencies with connecting lines (connectedness)\n   239â†’- Minimal chart decoration (closure)\n   240â†’- Chronological timeline (continuity)\n   241â†’\n   242â†’---\n   243â†’\n   244â†’## 7. Calendar Heatmap Patterns\n   245â†’\n   246â†’### When to Use\n   247â†’- Daily values are important\n   248â†’- Want to view whole year at once\n   249â†’- Spot patterns at month/week/day level\n   250â†’- Compare weekdays vs weekends\n   251â†’\n   252â†’### When NOT to Use\n   253â†’- Need trend/seasonality analysis (use line charts)\n   254â†’- Monthly/yearly aggregate trends\n   255â†’- Simple KPI tracking\n   256â†’\n   257â†’### Best Practices\n   258â†’- Choose readable colors even with dark shades\n   259â†’- Use continuous date calendar table\n   260â†’- Common layouts: 4x3, 3x4, 6x2 grids\n   261â†’- Color intensity = value magnitude\n   262â†’\n   263â†’### MMS Application\n   264â†’- **MACRO view**: Calendar heatmap showing maintenance density\n   265â†’- **Color scale**: Viridis (low=yellow â†’ high=purple)\n   266â†’- **Drill-down**: Click month/week to zoom MESO level\n   267â†’\n   268â†’---\n   269â†’\n   270â†’## 8. MMS Differentiators (vs Competitors)\n   271â†’\n   272â†’| Feature | Competitors | MMS |\n   273â†’|---------|-------------|-----|\n   274â†’| Zoom | Discrete tabs (day/week/month) | Continuous semantic zoom |\n   275â†’| Performance | Maximo struggles at 10k | 60fps with 60k tasks |\n   276â†’| Interface | Fragmented role-based apps | Unified single canvas |\n   277â†’| Conflict resolution | Manual identification | Visual drag-drop with instant feedback |\n   278â†’| Scenario mode | Separate planning modules | Inline sandbox with violet visual |\n   279â†’| Chart type | Gantt only | Gantt + Marey + Heatmap (LOD) |\n   280â†’\n   281â†’---\n   282â†’\n   283â†’## 9. Features to Steal (Priority Order)\n   284â†’\n   285â†’### HIGH PRIORITY\n   286â†’1. Drag-and-drop Gantt scheduling (IBM Maximo, Tractian)\n   287â†’2. Color-coded anomaly indicators (Railnova)\n   288â†’3. Event state pipeline (unplanned â†’ planned â†’ overdue â†’ closed)\n   289â†’4. Resource histogram below schedule (Maximo)\n   290â†’5. Mobile dispatch interface (Railnova)\n   304â†’\n   305â†’---\n   306â†’\n   307â†’## Sources\n   308â†’\n   309â†’- [IBM Maximo Scheduler - Prometheus Group](https://www.prometheusgroup.com/solutions/planning-and-scheduling-ibm-maximo)\n   310â†’- [Railnova Official Site](https://www.railnova.eu/)\n   311â†’- [Railfleet ECM Platform](https://www.railnova.eu/products/railfleet/)\n   312â†’- [SignatureRail Planning Solutions](https://signaturerail.com/planning-solutions/)\n   313â†’- [Marey's Trains - D3 Observable](https://observablehq.com/@d3/mareys-trains)\n   314â†’- [Gestalt Principles in Dashboard Design](https://playfairdata.com/applying-gestalt-principles-to-dashboard-design/)\n   315â†’- [WMATA Service Excellence Dashboard](https://www.wmata.com/initiatives/open-data-hub/Metro-Service-Excellence-Dashboard.cfm)\n   316â†’- [Tractian CMMS](https://tractian.com/en/cmms/planning-and-scheduling)\n   317â†’- [PTV Visum](https://www.ptvgroup.com/en-us/products/ptv-visum)\n   318â†’\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. IBM Maximo Scheduler\n\n### Overview\nIBM's enterprise asset management system, widely used in rail/metro operations including Dubai Metro.\n\n### Key UI Features\n\n#### Gantt Chart Capabilities\n- **Drag-and-drop scheduling**: Modify and mass-update work orders directly in Gantt view\n- **Task dependencies**: Visual critical path with float calculation\n- **Resource histogram**: Below Gantt showing resource loading vs availability\n- **Multiple views**: Day, week, month, quarter, year timeline views\n- **PM forecasting**: Gantt view for preventive maintenance with easy drag-drop adjustments\n\n#### Planning Views\n- **Graphical Scheduling**: Long to short term planning with Gantt + histogram\n- **Work View & Assignment View**: Both Gantt-based for supervisors\n- **Dispatch View**: Day calendar + map showing labor/crew assignments with travel time\n\n#### Railway-Specific Features\n- **Consist management**: For railway assets\n- **Dynamic segmentation**: For Railways, Roads, Pipelines\n- **Repair facility assignment**: Short-term planning for garage/railway shed assignment\n\n### Prometheus Group Enhancement (for Maximo)\n- **Four-quadrant Gantt layout**: Flexible view\n- **Real-time capacity effects**: See resource impact instantly\n- **Direct Edit or Mass Change**: For orders and operations\n\n### Weaknesses to Exploit\n- Fragmented into role-based apps (our opportunity: unified interface)\n- Struggles with 10k+ records (our target: 60fps with 60k tasks)\n- Discrete day/week/month tabs (our advantage: continuous semantic zoom)\n\n---\n\n## 2. Railnova (Brussels, Belgium)\n\n### Overview\nFleet management + telematics for rail industry. Clients include Eurotunnel, Alstom, Lineas, SNCF, DB Cargo.\n\n### Products\n\n#### Railfleet (ECM Fleet Management)\n- **Operational Dashboard**: All fleet info in one place\n  - Asset status\n  - Restrictions\n  - Rolling stock location\n  - Key telematic values\n  - Events and availability notifications\n\n- **Planning Dashboard**: Complete overview of maintenance events\n  - Event states: unplanned, planned, overdue, closed\n  - Per-asset filtering\n  - Bundle corrective + preventive into interventions\n\n- **Maintenance Planning Dashboard**:\n  - Filter events by type\n  - Schedule new maintenance interventions\n  - Bundle based on open events\n\n- **Digital Train Logbook**:\n  - Real-time tablet view for drivers\n  - Train availability and condition\n  - Operational status and restrictions\n  - Recent events list\n\n#### Railgenius (Real-time Monitoring)\n- **Live Monitoring Module**:\n  - Real-time telematics from all assets\n  - Color-coded values for anomaly detection\n  - Predictive alerts via detection rules\n\n- **Telematics Data Dashboard**:\n  - Historical and real-time data\n  - Usage pattern analysis\n  - Fault code occurrence reporting\n\n### Integration Capabilities\n- Web services API\n- IBM Maximo, MySQL Server, .Net, Oracle, SAP integration\n\n### UI Patterns to Steal\n- Color-coded anomaly indicators (spot abnormal values instantly)\n- Event state pipeline (unplanned â†’ planned â†’ overdue â†’ closed)\n- Digital logbook concept for train-level status\n- Bundling corrective + preventive events\n\n---\n\n## 3. SignatureRail (UK, Modaxo Group)\n\n### Overview\nTrain timetable and resource planning since 1994. Global presence with TrainPlan flagship product.\n\n### TrainPlan Features\n- Timetable generation considering all resources and constraints\n- Optimize capacity\n- Reduce vehicle and operator costs\n- Improve services\n\n### User Testimonial (Metro-North Railroad)\n> \"TrainPlan allows us to manage our data in an efficient manner allowing for safe and high performing service changes to be implemented as quickly as possible.\"\n\n### Marey Chart Visualization (Industry Gold Standard)\n\n#### What is a Marey Chart?\n- Invented by أ‰tienne-Jules Marey (1878) based on Mr. Ibry's method\n- **Axes**: Distance (Y) vs Time (X)\n- **Trains**: Diagonal lines - slope = speed\n- **Direction**: Top-left to bottom-right (one direction), bottom-left to top-right (reverse)\n- **Stops**: Horizontal sections indicate stop duration\n- **Crossings**: Lines crossing = trains passing each other\n\n#### Why Marey Charts are Superior\n- \"Far superior representation for finding late trains and understanding delay propagation\"\n- Both time and space dimensions in compact form\n- Used as cover of Tufte's \"The Visual Display of Quantitative Information\"\n- Speed immediately visible from line slope\n\n#### Modern Implementations\n- [D3.js Observable Example](https://observablehq.com/@d3/mareys-trains)\n- [JointJS Demo](https://www.jointjs.com/demos/marey-chart)\n- [Tableau Public](https://public.tableau.com/en-us/s/blog/2015/08/marey-inspired-path-train-schedule)\n\n### UI Patterns to Steal\n- Marey chart for train scheduling visualization\n- What-if scenario management\n- Constraint-aware scheduling\n\n---\n\n## 4. Other Notable Competitors\n\n### PTV Visum\n- **Scenario management**: Create and manage infrastructure modifications\n- **Fleet planning**: Depot capacities, turning times, empty runs\n- **Batch evaluation**: Compare scenarios programmatically\n\n### TBEST (Free Desktop Software)\n- **GIS-based**: Integrates socio-economic, land use, transit network\n- **Scenario planning**: True network-based future year simulation\n\n### AnyLogic\n- **Simulation-based optimization**: Two-layer approach\n- **What-if scenarios**: Assess economic effects of changes\n- **Replacement service planning**: For scheduled shutdowns\n\n### Tractian CMMS\n- **AI-driven workload balancing**: Eliminates scheduling conflicts\n- **Real-time technician availability**: Auto task prioritization\n- **Visual Scheduling**: Plan by technician, area, or shift\n- **Overload detection**: See idle time instantly\n\n### FaultFixers\n- **Drag-and-drop calendar**: Intuitive ticket assignment\n- **Gantt Chart view**: See everyone's schedule at once\n\n---\n\n## 5. Transit Dashboard Best Practices\n\n### Real-World Examples\n\n#### WMATA (Washington Metro)\n- **MetroPulse**: Real-time headway and schedule adherence\n- **Trains in service**: Live count\n- **Service Excellence Dashboard**: Rail, Bus, MetroAccess data\n\n#### CTE KPI Platform\n- **Single pane of glass**: Track progress, spot trends\n- **Automated API feeds**: Vehicle telematics integration\n- **Custom data inputs**: Flexible reporting\n\n### Common Transit KPIs\n- Miles driven\n- Drop sequence\n- Number of stops\n- Arrival/departure times\n- DOT reportable incidents\n- Speeding incidents\n- Cost-per-mile\n- Revenue-per-mile\n- Miles per gallon\n- Vehicle idle time\n- On-time performance\n- Missed trips\n- Prediction accuracy\n\n---\n\n## 6. Gestalt Principles for Dashboard Design\n\n### Core Principles\n\n1. **Proximity**\n   - Group related filters together\n   - Place filter near its affected view\n   - Example: Fleet status filters grouped with fleet list\n\n2. **Similarity**\n   - Same colors for same data types\n   - Consistent shapes for similar metrics\n   - Example: All \"fit\" trains in green, all \"unfit\" in red\n\n3. **Enclosure**\n   - Cards/boxes for related metrics\n   - Clear distinction between data sets\n   - Example: KPI cards with distinct borders\n\n4. **Connectedness**\n   - Lines linking related elements\n   - Network and line graph patterns\n   - Example: Dependency lines between tasks\n\n5. **Closure**\n   - Minimal axis lines (just X and Y enough)\n   - User perceives complete structure\n   - Example: Bar charts without full enclosure\n\n6. **Continuity**\n   - Sort bars large to small\n   - Time charts past to future\n   - Perceived as continuous whole\n\n### Application to MMS\n- Group maintenance metrics together (proximity)\n- Color-code by fleet type and task status (similarity)\n- Use cards for KPIs with clear borders (enclosure)\n- Show task dependencies with connecting lines (connectedness)\n- Minimal chart decoration (closure)\n- Chronological timeline (continuity)\n\n---\n\n## 7. Calendar Heatmap Patterns\n\n### When to Use\n- Daily values are important\n- Want to view whole year at once\n- Spot patterns at month/week/day level\n- Compare weekdays vs weekends\n\n### When NOT to Use\n- Need trend/seasonality analysis (use line charts)\n- Monthly/yearly aggregate trends\n- Simple KPI tracking\n\n### Best Practices\n- Choose readable colors even with dark shades\n- Use continuous date calendar table\n- Common layouts: 4x3, 3x4, 6x2 grids\n- Color intensity = value magnitude\n\n### MMS Application\n- **MACRO view**: Calendar heatmap showing maintenance density\n- **Color scale**: Viridis (low=yellow â†’ high=purple)\n- **Drill-down**: Click month/week to zoom MESO level\n\n---\n\n## 8. MMS Differentiators (vs Competitors)\n\n| Feature | Competitors | MMS |\n|---------|-------------|-----|\n| Zoom | Discrete tabs (day/week/month) | Continuous semantic zoom |\n| Performance | Maximo struggles at 10k | 60fps with 60k tasks |\n| Interface | Fragmented role-based apps | Unified single canvas |\n| Conflict resolution | Manual identification | Visual drag-drop with instant feedback |\n| Scenario mode | Separate planning modules | Inline sandbox with violet visual |\n| Chart type | Gantt only | Gantt + Marey + Heatmap (LOD) |\n\n---\n\n## 9. Features to Steal (Priority Order)\n\n### HIGH PRIORITY\n1. Drag-and-drop Gantt scheduling (IBM Maximo, Tractian)\n2. Color-coded anomaly indicators (Railnova)\n3. Event state pipeline (unplanned â†’ planned â†’ overdue â†’ closed)\n4. Resource histogram below schedule (Maximo)\n5. Marey chart for train visualization (SignatureRail)\n\n### MEDIUM PRIORITY\n6. Digital train logbook per asset (Railnova)\n7. Four-quadrant flexible layout (Prometheus)\n8. Scenario comparison with batch evaluation (PTV Visum)\n9. What-if scenario with economic impact (AnyLogic)\n10. Calendar heatmap for maintenance density\n\n### FUTURE CONSIDERATION\n11. AI-driven workload balancing (Tractian)\n12. GIS integration (TBEST)\n13. Predictive maintenance alerts (Railgenius)\n14.
